---
name: ddx
description: Operates the DDx toolkit for document-driven development. Covers beads (work items), the queue, executions, agents, harnesses, personas, reviews, spec-id. Use when the user says "do work", "drain the queue", "run the next bead", "execute a bead", "review this", "check against spec", "what's on the queue", "what's ready", "create a bead", "file this as work", "run an agent", "dispatch", "use a persona", "how am I doing", "ddx doctor", or mentions any ddx CLI command.
---

# DDx

DDx (Document-Driven Development eXperience) is a CLI platform for
document-driven development. It ships a bead tracker (portable work
items with acceptance criteria), an agent service boundary (DDx
orchestrates work while the upstream agent routes providers/models), a persona system (bindable AI
personalities), a library registry (plugins with prompts, templates,
personas), and git-aware synchronization. This skill makes any
skills-compatible coding agent (Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Gemini
CLI, etc.) understand and operate the DDx surface correctly.

## How this skill works

The skill body you're reading is an **overview** plus an **intent
router**. The real domain guidance lives in `reference/*.md` files.

**Directive: before responding to any DDx-related request, read the
matching reference file from the router table below. The router is
not optional — your answer must be grounded in the reference file's
guidance, not this overview alone.**

## Vocabulary

Single source of truth for DDx terms. Every reference file uses these
exact definitions.

- **Bead** — a portable work item (task, bug, epic, chore) with
  metadata, dependencies, and acceptance criteria. `ddx bead create`.
- **Queue** — the set of open beads. The *ready queue* is the subset
  with all dependencies closed. `ddx bead ready`, `ddx bead blocked`.
- **Ready** — a bead whose dependencies are all closed and which is
  eligible to be picked up next. `ddx bead ready`.
- **Blocked** — a bead with at least one unclosed dependency.
  `ddx bead blocked`.
- **Claim** — mark a bead as in-progress by an agent (concurrent-write
  protection). `ddx bead update <id> --claim`.
- **Close** — mark a bead as done, with evidence (session, commit
  SHA). `ddx bead close <id>`. Beads only close on execution outcomes
  `success` or `already_satisfied`.
- **Run** — one agent invocation atom. `ddx run` calls upstream agent
  `Execute` once with prompt/config, `MinPower`/`MaxPower` bounds, and optional
  passthrough constraints.
- **Try** — one bead attempt in an isolated worktree. `ddx try <id>` wraps
  `ddx run` with bead prompt resolution, evidence capture, and merge/preserve
  finalization.
- **Work** — drain the bead queue. `ddx work` picks ready beads and invokes
  `ddx try`; it owns queue iteration and retry policy.
- **Execution** — a generic DDx execution run (FEAT-010). Includes execution
  definitions, execution records, and execution evidence under `.ddx/runs/<id>/`.
- **Agent** — an AI coding agent (Claude, Codex, Gemini, etc.)
  invoked via a harness. Not a subagent (harness-specific — see
  below).
- **Harness** — an upstream agent routing concept. DDx may pass `--harness` as
  an operator-supplied constraint, but DDx does not validate, rank, fallback, or
  branch on harness names.
- **Persona** — a Markdown file (YAML frontmatter + body) that
  defines an AI personality. DDx injects the body as a system-prompt
  addendum to `ddx run`. `ddx persona list/show/bind`.
- **Role** — an abstract function (e.g., `code-reviewer`,
  `test-engineer`) a workflow can reference. Projects bind roles to
  personas.
- **Binding** — a project-specific `role: persona` map in
  `.ddx/config.yaml` under `persona_bindings`.
- **Power bounds** — `MinPower` and optional `MaxPower` integers passed to the
  upstream agent. DDx may raise `MinPower` on eligible retries; the agent owns
  the concrete harness/provider/model routing.
- **Plugin** — a self-contained extension installed to
  `.ddx/plugins/<name>/`. The default `ddx` plugin (personas,
  prompts, patterns, templates) is auto-installed by `ddx init`.
  `ddx install <name>`.
- **Skill** — an agentskills.io-standard directory (SKILL.md +
  optional `reference/`, `evals/`, `scripts/`). This `ddx` skill is
  the one DDx ships. Plugins can ship additional skills.
- **Subagent** — a harness-local concept for running a prompt in an
  isolated context (Claude Code's `.claude/agents/` + `context:
  fork`; Codex's `agents/`; others differ). DDx does not specify subagent
  orchestration; that remains harness business.
- **Update** — refresh plugin/toolkit content to a newer version.
  `ddx update [<plugin>]`.
- **Upgrade** — replace the DDx binary with a newer release.
  `ddx upgrade`.
- **Review** — two distinct concepts. **Bead review**
  (`ddx bead review <id>`) grades a completed bead against its
  acceptance criteria. **Comparison/adversarial review** is a workflow skill
  composition over `ddx run`, not a core quorum flag. See `reference/review.md`.
- **Governing artifact** — the document that authorizes a bead's
  work: a FEAT-\*, SD-\*, TD-\*, or ADR-\* under `docs/`. Referenced
  via `spec-id`.
- **Spec-id** — the `spec-id: <ID>` custom field on a bead pointing
  at its governing artifact.

## Intent router

Before responding, read the matching file.

| User says / asks about | Read this file |
|---|---|
| write/plan work, "create a bead", "file this as work", bead metadata, acceptance criteria, dependencies | `reference/beads.md` |
| "do work", "drain the queue", "run the next bead", "execute a bead", "run work", verify-and-close | `reference/work.md` |
| "review this", "check against spec", bead review, quorum review, code review, adversarial check | `reference/review.md` |
| "score a bead", "triage a failed attempt", "refine a bead", bead authoring lint | `bead-lifecycle/` |
| "run an agent", "dispatch", harness/provider/model passthrough, power, effort, "use a persona", role bindings | `reference/agents.md` |
| "what's on the queue", "what's ready", "how am I doing", health check, "ddx doctor", sync status | `reference/status.md` |

If the intent spans multiple files (e.g., "create a bead and then
run it"), read beads.md first, then work.md. If no match, ask the
user which concept they mean rather than guessing.

## Top-level policy reminders

These apply across all DDx operations. Do not restate them in every
reference file; do not violate them.

- **Never edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl` directly.** All tracker changes go
  through `ddx bead create/update/close/dep`. Direct edits corrupt
  bead history and cannot be audited.
- **Tracker changes are commit-worthy.** After `ddx bead create`,
  `update`, `dep add/remove`, or `close`, commit the resulting
  `.ddx/beads.jsonl` change — either as a tracker-only commit or
  folded into the same commit as related implementation changes.
- **Preserve bead-attempt commit history.** Branches containing
  `ddx try` / `ddx work` execution commits carry an audit trail. **Never
  squash, rebase, filter, or amend** these commits. Use only
  `git merge --ff-only` or `git merge --no-ff` when merging.
  `gh pr merge --squash` and `--rebase` are forbidden on these
  branches.
- **Work in worktrees for parallel agents.** Use `wt switch -c
  <branch>` (worktrunk) or equivalent to give each concurrent agent
  its own isolated checkout. Execute-bead does this automatically;
  manual parallel work should too.
- **Power-first agent dispatch.** Default to `ddx run`/`ddx try`/`ddx work`
  with power bounds. `--harness`, `--provider`, and `--model` are passthrough
  constraints only; DDx must not use them for routing policy.

## Links out

- Full CLI reference: `ddx --help`, `ddx <subcommand> --help`.
- Governing feature specs: see `FEAT-*` documents under your
  project's `docs/` tree — especially the CLI, beads, agent-service,
  executions, and skills features.
- Personas README: shipped by the default `ddx` plugin at
  `.ddx/plugins/ddx/personas/README.md`.
- Open standard this skill conforms to:
  [agentskills.io](https://agentskills.io).

guidance, not this overview alone.**

## Vocabulary

Single source of truth for DDx terms. Every reference file uses these
exact definitions.

- **Bead** — a portable work item (task, bug, epic, chore) with
  metadata, dependencies, and acceptance criteria. `ddx bead create`.
- **Queue** — the set of open beads. The *ready queue* is the subset
  with all dependencies closed. `ddx bead ready`, `ddx bead blocked`.
- **Ready** — a bead whose dependencies are all closed and which is
  eligible to be picked up next. `ddx bead ready`.
- **Blocked** — a bead with at least one unclosed dependency.
  `ddx bead blocked`.
- **Claim** — mark a bead as in-progress by an agent (concurrent-write
  protection). `ddx bead update <id> --claim`.
- **Close** — mark a bead as done, with evidence (session, commit
  SHA). `ddx bead close <id>`. Beads only close on execution outcomes
  `success` or `already_satisfied`.
- **Run** — one agent invocation atom. `ddx run` calls upstream agent
  `Execute` once with prompt/config, `MinPower`/`MaxPower` bounds, and optional
  passthrough constraints.
- **Try** — one bead attempt in an isolated worktree. `ddx try <id>` wraps
  `ddx run` with bead prompt resolution, evidence capture, and merge/preserve
  finalization.
- **Work** — drain the bead queue. `ddx work` picks ready beads and invokes
  `ddx try`; it owns queue iteration and retry policy.
- **Execution** — a generic DDx execution run (FEAT-010). Includes execution
  definitions, execution records, and execution evidence under `.ddx/runs/<id>/`.
- **Agent** — an AI coding agent (Claude, Codex, Gemini, etc.)
  invoked via a harness. Not a subagent (harness-specific — see
  below).
- **Harness** — an upstream agent routing concept. DDx may pass `--harness` as
  an operator-supplied constraint, but DDx does not validate, rank, fallback, or
  branch on harness names.
- **Persona** — a Markdown file (YAML frontmatter + body) that
  defines an AI personality. DDx injects the body as a system-prompt
  addendum to `ddx run`. `ddx persona list/show/bind`.
- **Role** — an abstract function (e.g., `code-reviewer`,
  `test-engineer`) a workflow can reference. Projects bind roles to
  personas.
- **Binding** — a project-specific `role: persona` map in
  `.ddx/config.yaml` under `persona_bindings`.
- **Power bounds** — `MinPower` and optional `MaxPower` integers passed to the
  upstream agent. DDx may raise `MinPower` on eligible retries; the agent owns
  the concrete harness/provider/model routing.
- **Plugin** — a self-contained extension installed to
  `.ddx/plugins/<name>/`. The default `ddx` plugin (personas,
  prompts, patterns, templates) is auto-installed by `ddx init`.
  `ddx install <name>`.
- **Skill** — an agentskills.io-standard directory (SKILL.md +
  optional `reference/`, `evals/`, `scripts/`). This `ddx` skill is
  the one DDx ships. Plugins can ship additional skills.
- **Subagent** — a harness-local concept for running a prompt in an
  isolated context (Claude Code's `.claude/agents/` + `context:
  fork`; Codex's `agents/`; others differ). DDx does not specify subagent
  orchestration; that remains harness business.
- **Update** — refresh plugin/toolkit content to a newer version.
  `ddx update [<plugin>]`.
- **Upgrade** — replace the DDx binary with a newer release.
  `ddx upgrade`.
- **Review** — two distinct concepts. **Bead review**
  (`ddx bead review <id>`) grades a completed bead against its
  acceptance criteria. **Comparison/adversarial review** is a workflow skill
  composition over `ddx run`, not a core quorum flag. See `reference/review.md`.
- **Governing artifact** — the document that authorizes a bead's
  work: a FEAT-\*, SD-\*, TD-\*, or ADR-\* under `docs/`. Referenced
  via `spec-id`.
- **Spec-id** — the `spec-id: <ID>` custom field on a bead pointing
  at its governing artifact.

## Intent router

Before responding, read the matching file.

| User says / asks about | Read this file |
|---|---|
| write/plan work, "create a bead", "file this as work", bead metadata, acceptance criteria, dependencies | `reference/beads.md` |
| "do work", "drain the queue", "run the next bead", "execute a bead", "run work", verify-and-close | `reference/work.md` |
| "review this", "check against spec", bead review, quorum review, code review, adversarial check | `reference/review.md` |
| "score a bead", "triage a failed attempt", "refine a bead", bead authoring lint | `bead-lifecycle/` |
| "run an agent", "dispatch", harness/provider/model passthrough, power, effort, "use a persona", role bindings | `reference/agents.md` |
| "what's on the queue", "what's ready", "how am I doing", health check, "ddx doctor", sync status | `reference/status.md` |

If the intent spans multiple files (e.g., "create a bead and then
run it"), read beads.md first, then work.md. If no match, ask the
user which concept they mean rather than guessing.

## Top-level policy reminders

These apply across all DDx operations. Do not restate them in every
reference file; do not violate them.

- **Never edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl` directly.** All tracker changes go
  through `ddx bead create/update/close/dep`. Direct edits corrupt
  bead history and cannot be audited.
- **Tracker changes are commit-worthy.** After `ddx bead create`,
  `update`, `dep add/remove`, or `close`, commit the resulting
  `.ddx/beads.jsonl` change — either as a tracker-only commit or
  folded into the same commit as related implementation changes.
- **Preserve bead-attempt commit history.** Branches containing
  `ddx try` / `ddx work` execution commits carry an audit trail. **Never
  squash, rebase, filter, or amend** these commits. Use only
  `git merge --ff-only` or `git merge --no-ff` when merging.
  `gh pr merge --squash` and `--rebase` are forbidden on these
  branches.
- **Work in worktrees for parallel agents.** Use `wt switch -c
  <branch>` (worktrunk) or equivalent to give each concurrent agent
  its own isolated checkout. Execute-bead does this automatically;
  manual parallel work should too.
- **Power-first agent dispatch.** Default to `ddx run`/`ddx try`/`ddx work`
  with power bounds. `--harness`, `--provider`, and `--model` are passthrough
  constraints only; DDx must not use them for routing policy.

## Links out

- Full CLI reference: `ddx --help`, `ddx <subcommand> --help`.
- Governing feature specs: see `FEAT-*` documents under your
  project's `docs/` tree — especially the CLI, beads, agent-service,
  executions, and skills features.
- Personas README: shipped by the default `ddx` plugin at
  `.ddx/plugins/ddx/personas/README.md`.
- Open standard this skill conforms to:
  [agentskills.io](https://agentskills.io).

I’m now locating the run-detail side and any existing artifact/session storage conventions so the review-session contract matches the repo’s current patterns instead of inventing a parallel one.

---
ddx:
  id: TD-033
  depends_on:
    - FEAT-022
    - FEAT-006
    - FEAT-008
  status: draft
---
# Technical Design: Multi-Turn Structured Evidence Assembly

## Purpose

This TD defines the architecture for assembling large DDx prompts in a
structured, multi-turn way. FEAT-022 defines the evidence caps and
bounded-assembly invariants; this TD defines how callers stage evidence,
resolve the assembly mode, and render a final prompt without each surface
inventing its own topology.

The design exists for three callers that need consistent behavior:

- the agent service boundary in FEAT-006
- the UI-triggered review flow in FEAT-008
- the bounded review and grading prompts in FEAT-022

The goal is not to introduce a single monolithic builder. The goal is to
standardize the shape of the assembly process so the review path, grading
path, and primary invocation path can share the same evidence vocabulary
while still choosing different top-level ordering and budgets.

## Current State

Today FEAT-022 already requires bounded prompt assembly, minimum evidence
floors, and a no-unbounded-prompts invariant. What is still missing is a
documented topology for how a caller moves from raw evidence to the final
rendered prompt when the request is review-like and tool-free.

That gap shows up in three places:

- FEAT-006 owns the request boundary but does not yet name the review-mode
  assembly shape.
- FEAT-008 exposes an "Agent review" action but does not yet say that the
  resulting session is tool-free by convention.
- FEAT-022 defines the caps and the section content rules but intentionally
  stops short of mandating a repo-wide `EvidenceAssembler` object.

## Design

### 1. Structured evidence envelope

Every assembly run begins with a small structured envelope that records:

- intent: `primary`, `review`, or `grading`
- mode: `ref-only` or `inline-with-cap`
- target budget: the applicable cap for the chosen intent
- evidence source set: bead metadata, docs, diffs, runs, or prompt bodies

The envelope is not the final prompt. It is the contract that lets the caller
plan the assembly without knowing the full content shape up front.

### 2. Multi-turn assembly phases

The assembly proceeds in three phases:

1. Plan the evidence set and section priority order.
2. Materialize the sections with the FEAT-022 cap primitives.
3. Render the final prompt and record the byte accounting.

This can happen in one process call, but it is still a multi-turn
architecture conceptually because the caller first chooses the evidence plan
and then performs the final render against that plan.

### 3. No-tool reviewer mode

Review-mode sessions do not receive tools. The caller hands the reviewer a
structured evidence envelope plus the rendered prompt, and the reviewer is
expected to reason over the supplied content only.

That convention matters because the review prompt is meant to be stable and
auditable. If a reviewer needs additional evidence, the caller must assemble it
up front rather than relying on a later tool call.

### 4. Caller-specific ordering

The shared envelope does not force a single assembly order across all call
sites.

- FEAT-022 review and grading paths keep their own section priorities.
- FEAT-006 keeps request-level intent and evidence capture ownership.
- FEAT-008 surfaces the review action and dispatches the session in the
  no-tool reviewer mode described here.

## Non-Goals

- This TD does not replace FEAT-022's cap primitives.
- This TD does not define new routing policy.
- This TD does not introduce map-reduce per-file review or any other
  parallel fan-out architecture.
- This TD does not require a new repository-wide assembler type.

---
ddx:
  id: TD-026
  depends_on:
    - FEAT-007
    - FEAT-010
    - TD-025
---
# Technical Design: Artifact Graph Schema

## Purpose

This design defines the graph record shape used after artifact identity expands
beyond markdown documents. It complements TD-025, which defines how artifact
identity is discovered from embedded frontmatter and sidecar `.ddx.yaml` files.

The graph must expose `media_type`, represent `generated_by` as a provenance
edge, and keep generated-artifact staleness separate from ordinary dependency
staleness.

## Graph Nodes

Each discovered artifact produces one graph node:

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `id` | string | Stable artifact id from `ddx.id`. |
| `path` | string | Repository-relative path to the artifact bytes or markdown file. |
| `identity_source` | enum | `frontmatter` for markdown, `sidecar` for non-markdown media. |
| `sidecar_path` | string? | Repository-relative sidecar path when `identity_source=sidecar`. |
| `media_type` | string | IANA-style media type. Markdown defaults to `text/markdown`; sidecars should set it explicitly and may be inferred only as a compatibility fallback. |
| `hash` | string | Current content hash. Markdown excludes the managed `review` block; sidecar artifacts hash paired artifact bytes. |
| `review` | object? | Last reviewed hash/dependency map written by `ddx doc stamp`. |
| `generated_by` | object? | `{ run, prompt, source_hash }` provenance copied from identity metadata. |

Graph APIs must preserve unknown identity fields for detail views, but graph
construction only interprets the fields above plus `depends_on`.

## Edge Types

The graph has two first-class edge types:

| Edge type | Source | Target | Authority? | Staleness behavior |
|-----------|--------|--------|------------|--------------------|
| `depends_on` | Artifact id | Upstream artifact id | Yes | Participates in review hash comparison and cascade propagation. |
| `generated_by` | Artifact id | Run id | No | Provenance only. Never cascades to dependents. |

`depends_on` edges are artifact-to-artifact edges. `generated_by` edges are
artifact-to-run edges that point into the unified FEAT-010 run substrate. A run
record that produced an artifact should also carry `produces_artifact` so the
relationship can be traversed in both directions.

No other core edge types are introduced by this design. Workflow-specific
relationships remain plugin or skill concerns.

## Staleness Model

Each node exposes separate staleness channels:

| Field | Meaning |
|-------|---------|
| `dependency_stale` | One or more `depends_on` targets changed since this artifact was stamped. |
| `cascade_stale` | An upstream artifact is stale and cascade propagation reaches this node. |
| `generated_stale` | `generated_by.source_hash` does not match the current hash of the generator inputs. |
| `missing_dependency` | A `depends_on` target is absent from the graph. |
| `missing_generator_run` | `generated_by.run` does not resolve in FEAT-010 run history. |

Only `dependency_stale`, `cascade_stale`, and `missing_dependency` participate
in authority staleness. `generated_stale` is local to the generated artifact
and drives explicit regeneration UI/CLI affordances. It must not mark
downstream `depends_on` artifacts stale by itself.

## Source Hash Comparison

Generated artifacts compare `generated_by.source_hash` against the current hash
of the generator inputs. The input set is resolved by the artifact generator or
run record, not guessed from graph topology:

1. If the producing run record includes a source-input hash, use that source.
2. If the sidecar names a prompt/source artifact, compute the hash over that
   artifact and any generator-declared inputs.
3. If inputs cannot be resolved, report `generated_stale=unknown` and keep the
   artifact indexed.

DDx never treats `generated_by.prompt` as an authority edge unless the artifact
also declares that prompt or source artifact in `depends_on`.

## API Shape

Read APIs should expose nodes and edges without forcing callers to infer
provenance from raw sidecar YAML:

```json
{
  "nodes": [
    {
      "id": "DIAG-001",
      "path": "docs/diagrams/flow.excalidraw",
      "identity_source": "sidecar",
      "sidecar_path": "docs/diagrams/flow.excalidraw.ddx.yaml",
      "media_type": "application/vnd.excalidraw+json",
      "hash": "sha256:...",
      "staleness": {
        "dependency_stale": false,
        "cascade_stale": false,
        "generated_stale": true,
        "missing_dependency": false,
        "missing_generator_run": false
      },
      "generated_by": {
        "run": "run-20260429T221533Z-a1b2c3d4",
        "prompt": "PROMPT-visual-suite-principles",
        "source_hash": "sha256:..."
      }
    }
  ],
  "edges": [
    {"from": "DIAG-001", "to": "FEAT-007", "type": "depends_on"},
    {"from": "DIAG-001", "to": "run-20260429T221533Z-a1b2c3d4", "type": "generated_by"}
  ]
}
```

GraphQL and HTTP field names should use the same semantics even if casing
differs by transport.

## Validation

`ddx doc validate` reports graph-schema issues with these severities:

| Condition | Severity |
|-----------|----------|
| Duplicate artifact id across frontmatter and sidecars | error |
| Sidecar missing paired artifact | error |
| Sidecar paired with markdown file | error |
| Sidecar missing explicit `media_type` | warning, with inferred value |
| Unknown `depends_on` target | warning |
| Unknown `generated_by.run` target | warning |
| Unresolvable generated source inputs | warning |

Validation must keep indexing valid nodes even when provenance cannot be fully
resolved, because provenance is diagnostic rather than authority.

Updated\\n    ddx-b808df39 notes to cross-reference this investigation so the\\n    operator stop/kill tool tracks the underlying fixes.\\n\\ndiff --git a/.ddx/executions/20260418T043148-1346e8a3/incident_report.md b/.ddx/executions/20260418T043148-1346e8a3/incident_report.md\\nnew file mode 100644\\nindex 00000000..5e8547cf\\n--- /dev/null\\n+++ b/.ddx/executions/20260418T043148-1346e8a3/incident_report.md\\n@@ -0,0 +1,193 @@\\n+# Investigation: root cause of 33–142h hung execute-bead workers\\n+\\n+**Bead:** ddx-0a651925 (investigates; sibling to ddx-b808df39)\\n+**Attempt:** 20260418T043148-1346e8a3\\n+**Scope:** Source-code + execution-bundle analysis. No live hung worker was available\\n+in this isolated worktree to capture live `ps`/`pprof`, so the incident evidence is\\n+drawn from `.ddx/executions/*` bundles and the worker/runner source path.\\n+\\n+---\\n+\\n+## 1. Observed evidence (from `.ddx/executions/`)\\n+\\n+| Metric                                               | Count / value                                        |\\n+| ---------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------- |\\n+| Total attempt bundles                                | 328                                                  |\\n+| Bundles with `result.json`                           | 291 (89%)                                            |\\n+| Bundles with `manifest.json` but **no** `result.json` | 37 (11%) — executions whose worker never finalized  |\\n+\\n+Top five longest *finalized* attempts:\\n+\\n+| `duration_ms` | Wall time    | `detail`                                                                    | Bundle                          |\\n+| ------------- | ------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------- |\\n+| 7,720,908     | **2h 8m**    | `agent: compaction stuck: 50 consecutive failed compaction attempts`        | 20260416T230316-efbf91c5        |\\n+| 7,200,098     | **2h 0m**    | `timeout after 2h0m0s` (claude streaming wall-clock fired)                  | 20260415T015231-c2ffa9ba        |\\n+| 6,764,927     | **1h 53m**   | success (agent harness, qwen)                                               | 20260416T230315-f9f46822        |\\n+| 5,649,987     | **1h 34m**   | compaction stuck                                                            | 20260416T230313-e193942b        |\\n+| 4,284,347     | **1h 11m**   | compaction stuck                                                            | 20260415T103422-bb3a45fa        |\\n+\\n+**Key observation:** the 33h / 142h hang durations cited in ddx-b808df39 are\\n+~15× and ~65× longer than the longest *finalized* attempt on record. This means\\n+the hung workers never reached the point of writing `result.json` — they were\\n+blocked inside `runner.Run` (or deeper) for days. Every code path that\\n+*should* have terminated them (idle timeout, compaction-stuck, stall detector,\\n+cancel propagation) failed to fire.\\n+\\n+The 37 manifest-only bundles are candidates for hung or externally-killed\\n+workers; e.g. `20260416T215953-6b515c05` has a manifest from 2026-04-16T21:59Z\\n+with no result. Those fit the profile of \\\"workers running 33h–142h with no bead\\n+assigned\\\" reported on agent projects.\\n+\\n+---\\n+\\n+## 2. Root-cause classification (source code evidence)\\n+\\n+The investigation checked the four hypotheses from the bead description.\\n+\\n+### RC1 (PRIMARY) — Caller context is discarded inside the Runner\\n+\\n+`cli/internal/agent/runner.go:239` (`Runner.Run`):\\n+```go\\n+ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\\n+```\\n+\\n+`cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:163` (`Runner.RunAgent`):\\n+```go\\n+ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\\n+```\\n+\\n+`RunOptions` (types.go) takes no `context.Context` at all. `agent.ExecuteBead`\\n+likewise does not accept ctx, and `server.WorkerManager.runWorker` passes its\\n+own ctx into `worker.Run(ctx, …)` but that ctx **never reaches the agent\\n+invocation**.\\n+\\n+**Impact:** `WorkerManager.Stop(id)` (workers.go:648) calls `handle.cancel()`\\n+which cancels only the worker goroutine's ctx. The running agent provider call\\n+/ subprocess is detached. The only way to interrupt a stuck agent today is a\\n+SIGKILL from outside the process. This is the structural defect that enables\\n+33–142h hangs — once the agent is stuck, nothing inside DDx can stop it.\\n+\\n+### RC2 — Idle-reset timer, not wall-clock deadline\\n+\\n+`cli/internal/agent/executor.go:35-40` creates an *idle timeout* context value:\\n+```go\\n+func withExecutionTimeout(ctx context.Context, timeout time.Duration) context.Context {\\n+    return context.WithValue(ctx, executionTimeoutKey{}, timeout)\\n+}\\n+```\\n+\\n+`executor.go:154-178` consumes it and **resets the timer on every stdout/stderr\\n+byte**. `agent_runner.go:264-285` implements the same pattern against agentlib\\n+events.\\n+\\n+The default timeout is 2h (`types.go:363 DefaultTimeoutMS = 7200000`). The\\n+comment claims \\\"2 hours — long enough for any task; supervisor/agent decides\\n+termination\\\" but the actual behaviour is **\\\"2 hours of silence required to fire\\\"**.\\n+A provider that emits a heartbeat, a ping, a retry line, or any byte resets the\\n+timer indefinitely.\\n+\\n+The `runClaudeStreaming` path (`claude_stream.go:373-384`) is the only path that\\n+uses a wall-clock deadline — and it is the only path we see actually hitting\\n+the timeout (bundle 20260415T015231 exactly 2h 0m 0.098s).\\n+\\n+**Impact:** for `harness=agent` (qwen, minimax, openrouter, etc.), no wall-clock\\n+bound exists. 33–142h hangs are consistent with a provider that dribbles events\\n+slowly enough to defeat the idle reset.\\n+\\n+### RC3 — Compaction-stuck breaker fires late\\n+\\n+`cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:218-241` increments a counter on each no-op\\n+compaction event and trips at `stuckThreshold = maxConsecutiveCompactionFailures`\\n+(50 by default). On observed `efbf91c5` it took **2h 8m** to trip. If one\\n+iteration takes 3 minutes to produce a no-op compaction event, 50 events =\\n+~150 min — matches the observed tail.\\n+\\n+**Impact:** on its own, the breaker is too slow to be a useful hang-guard. It\\n+should be replaced or complemented by a wall-clock-bounded variant.\\n+\\n+### RC4 — Provider HTTP requests likely lack a per-request deadline\\n+\\n+`RunAgent` passes the internal `context.Background()`-derived ctx into\\n+`agentlib.Run(ctx, req)`. The agent library's providers (`openai.Client`,\\n+anthropic client) do not appear to install a per-request deadline themselves\\n+(grepping the v0.3.14 module cache shows short timeouts on discovery/probe\\n+only; the main call path has no WithTimeout). A stalled TCP socket after\\n+headers are received but before the response body arrives can hang a streaming\\n+read indefinitely.\\n+\\n+**Impact:** consistent with \\\"alive but mid-LLM-call with no client-side\\n+deadline\\\" — hypothesis (2) in the bead description. Cannot be fully confirmed\\n+from source alone; needs runtime `go tool pprof http://localhost:<port>/debug/pprof/goroutine`\\n+against a live hung worker.\\n+\\n+### RC-skipped — BashTool subprocess hang (hypothesis 1)\\n+\\n+Not the root cause. `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent@v0.3.14/tool/bash.go:15`\\n+installs a 120s default wall-clock timeout (`context.WithTimeout(ctx, timeout)`)\\n+and calls `exec.CommandContext`. .ddx/executions/20260413T153034-7d956f17/manifest.json:13:    "description": "\u003ccontext-digest\u003e\nAlpha validation run from /Users/erik/Projects/helix using `ddx agent execute-loop --once --json` against a live HELIX queue. Evidence includes the generated execution bundle `.ddx/executions/20260411T011535-bbda19d7`, worktree `.ddx/.execute-bead-wt-helix-fef22846-20260411T011535-bbda19d7`, and the current execute-loop implementation in `cli/internal/bead/store.go` and `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`.\n\u003c/context-digest\u003e\n\n`ddx agent execute-loop` selected and launched an open `epic` bead from the HELIX execution queue instead of a bounded executable bead.\n\n## Reproduction\n1. In `/Users/erik/Projects/helix`, run `ddx bead ready --json --execution`.\n2. Observe the first candidate is `helix-fef22846`, `issue_type=epic`, title `Audit deleted HELIX artifact types for restoration or retirement`.\n3. Run `ddx agent execute-loop --once --json`.\n4. Observe DDx creates `.ddx/.execute-bead-wt-helix-fef22846-20260411T011535-bbda19d7` and `.ddx/executions/20260411T011535-bbda19d7/manifest.json` with `bead_id=helix-fef22846`.\n\n## Observed gap\n`ReadyExecution()` currently treats any open, unblocked bead as execution-ready unless `execution-eligible=false`, `superseded-by`, or cooldown metadata says otherwise. `execute-loop` then takes the first candidate without a stronger structural execution screen. That behavior is weaker than the API-001 execute-bead supervisor contract, which says the loop should run a generic execution-ready validator against ordered candidates before launch.\n\n## Why this matters\nOpen epics represent umbrella planning/decomposition work, not necessarily a bounded workspace transform. Launching them directly makes queue-drain behavior unstable and can strand the loop on non-executable work even when bounded tasks are ready behind the epic.",

`ddx agent execute-loop` selected and launched an open `epic` bead from the HELIX execution queue instead of a bounded executable bead.

## Reproduction
1. In `/Users/erik/Projects/helix`, run `ddx bead ready --json --execution`.
2. Observe the first candidate is `helix-fef22846`, `issue_type=epic`, title `Audit deleted HELIX artifact types for restoration or retirement`.
3. Run `ddx agent execute-loop --once --json`.
4. Observe DDx creates `.ddx/.execute-bead-wt-helix-fef22846-20260411T011535-bbda19d7` and `.ddx/executions/20260411T011535-bbda19d7/manifest.json` with `bead_id=helix-fef22846`.

## Observed gap
`ReadyExecution()` currently treats any open, unblocked bead as execution-ready unless `execution-eligible=false`, `superseded-by`, or cooldown metadata says otherwise. `execute-loop` then takes the first candidate without a stronger structural execution screen. That behavior is weaker than the API-001 execute-bead supervisor contract, which says the loop should run a generic execution-ready validator against ordered candidates before launch.

## Why this matters
Open epics represent umbrella planning/decomposition work, not necessarily a bounded workspace transform. Launching them directly makes queue-drain behavior unstable and can strand the loop on non-executable work even when bounded tasks are ready behind the epic.

.ddx/executions/20260414T073103-1cebdc41/prompt.md:5:There is no CLI command to see which workers are running and which bead each is executing. The WorkerManager in cli/internal/server/workers.go already tracks this (WorkerRecord.CurrentBead at line 51, WorkerRecord.State, WorkerRecord.Harness, WorkerRecord.Model, WorkerRecord.Attempts/Successes/Failures) but it is only accessible through the server API. Local --local workers are not tracked by the server at all.

This bead adds ddx agent workers as a top-level CLI subcommand. It must not depend on worktrunk (wt) or any external tool.

## Required changes

### 1. New command: ddx agent workers (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go)
Register alongside the existing ddx agent log command. Flags:
  --json         emit raw JSON array
  --watch        re-render every 2s (like watch -n2) until Ctrl-C
  --project      filter to a specific project root (default: CWD project)

### 2. Data sources (two paths)
A. Server-submitted workers: GET http://127.0.0.1:8080/api/agent/workers — deserialize []WorkerRecord from the server. Already implemented in cli/internal/server/workers.go List() / HTTP handler.
B. Local --local workers: the server does not track these. Detect them by:
   1. Reading .ddx/workers/*/status.json files in the project root (server writes these even for local workers when they self-register — confirm in workers.go; if not, fall back to scanning active execute-bead worktrees)
   2. Scanning .ddx/.execute-bead-wt-* directories (from ExecuteBeadWtPrefix constant in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go line 403) and reading the manifest.json inside each to extract bead_id, attempt_id, harness, model, started_at

### 3. Local worker detection via manifest scan
For each directory matching .ddx/.execute-bead-wt-*:
  - Read .ddx/executions/{attemptID}/manifest.json (path encoded in worktree name suffix)
  - Check if worktree is still active (git worktree list --porcelain)
  - Read execute-loop-heartbeat-at from bead.Extra (requires ddx-7651506a) if available
  - Derive synthetic WorkerRecord from manifest fields

### 4. Table output (default)
Columns: STATE | BEAD | TITLE (truncated 40 chars) | HARNESS | MODEL | ELAPSED | ATTEMPTS
- STATE: running (green), exited (dim), failed (red)
- ELAPSED: human-readable (e.g., 4m32s)
- If current bead title unavailable (local worker pre-manifest), show bead ID

### 5. Merge and deduplicate
When both server and manifest sources are available, deduplicate by worker_id or attempt_id. Server record takes precedence over manifest-derived record for the same worker.

## Key files
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go — add newAgentWorkersCommand(), register in newAgentCommand()
- cli/internal/server/workers.go — WorkerRecord struct (lines 31-54), List API
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go — ExecuteBeadWtPrefix constant (line 403), manifest shape (lines 726-771)
- cli/internal/agent/types.go — SessionEntry Correlation map (bead_id field)

agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=15844 cost_usd=1.8110"},{"actor":"erik","body":"Bead scope significantly exceeds the stated \"~200 LOC + test updates\" estimate; safe execution requires decomposition into 3 child beads, but child-bead creation must originate from the main repo (not this execution worktree) to get clean ddx-prefixed IDs. ## Why this attempt declined to commit The bead asks for an end-to-end migration of the server-spawn path from in-process execution to subprocess `exec ddx work`. Six tightly-coupled ACs touch the most intricate file in the server package. ### Actual surface (vs stated estimate) cli/internal/server/workers.go is 1,959 lines. The current `runWorker` (lines 612–926, ~315 lines) is a dense in-process orchestrator that knits together: - in-process `agent.ExecuteBeadWorker` construction - per-project `LandCoordinator` single-flight serialization - in-process post-merge `DefaultBeadReviewer` - in-process `escalation.CostCapTracker` (per-worker billed cost accumulation) - in-process `RoutePreflight` (typed-incompatibility gate via FizeauService) - per-attempt `singleTierAttempt` closure with config resolution + gate evaluation + leftover-evidence safety net - progress channel piped to SSE subscribers via `drainProgress` - watchdog phase-stall tracking via shared workerHandle.lastPhaseTS - terminal-state preservation (stopped/reaped) merged with loop result - BeadWorkerFactory injection point for test fakes Replacing the in-process call with `exec ddx work` collapses all of those in-process couplings — they now run inside the child process — but the parent server still needs equivalent observability/control via: - /api/workers/{id}/event ingestion (already exists from step 1) for progress - PID + signal escalation for watchdog/Stop (PID field exists; signal plumbing is partial) - worker-events.jsonl tail or HTTP-mirrored events for SSE bridging - Cross-process Stop / wakeCh equivalent ### Test surface cli/internal/server/workers_test.go alone is 1,107 lines, plus 9 sibling workers_*_test.go files and graphql_subscription_worker_test.go that all assert in-process semantics. Many tests inject `BeadWorkerFactory` for control over the in-process worker; with subprocess execution, those tests need to be replaced by either: - a built-in subprocess-stub binary (e.g. `go test -test.run … -ddx-fake-work-stub` reentry pattern) the parent execs in tests, or - deletion as no-longer-applicable (semantics now belong to ddx work's own test surface). This is the bulk of the work, not \"test updates.\" ### AC #5 (real-harness end-to-end) \"server-spawned worker (via the new exec path) registers via the new endpoints AND successfully invokes the claude harness (real claude binary, not a stub) on a fixture bead AND completes the attempt with a commit\" Even with the fall-back to a scripted-claude-stub, this requires: a clean fixture repo with a real ready bead, a stub or real claude binary on $PATH inside the test harness, a server bound to a free port, the new exec path wired in, the worker registering against the running server's /api/workers/register, and an end-to-end commit landing through the LandCoordinator (which has moved out of the parent process). That is a substantial new integration test — comparable to the workers_e2e_test.go suite — distinct from the refactor proper. ### Size-check verdict - More than ~6 ACs across unrelated subsystems? 6 ACs, but several (in-process-replacement refactor, struct deletion, full test sweep, real-harness integration) are independently sized engineering deliverables. - AC mixes implementation, integration tests, and end-to-end scenarios? Yes (AC #1–4 = refactor, AC #5 = new integration test, AC #6 = full lefthook gate). - More than ~500 LOC across more than ~5 files? Yes — workers.go rewrite (~400), server.go handler (~70), workers_test.go sweep (~600+), sibling test sweep (~500+), new exec/stub plumbing (~200), new e2e integration test (~300). Estimated 1,500–2,500 LOC of changes. The bead's \"~200 LOC + test updates\" estimate (echoing the ADR-022 step 7 roadmap line) materially understates the work. ## Proposed decomposition (orchestrator should create from main repo) Create the following children of ddx-eccc6efb from the **main repo** (not from an execution worktree — IDs created from a worktree get the worktree dirname as the project prefix and become unusable): ### 7a — runWorker exec rewrite + parent-side subprocess plumbing Title: ADR-022 step 7a: workers.go runWorker → exec ddx work subprocess (progress via /api/workers/{id}/event, watchdog via PID) Scope: - Replace the in-process `worker.Run(...)` body in cli/internal/server/workers.go with `exec.CommandContext` of `ddx work` (resolved via os.Executable() + arg-vec flags + DDX_PROJECT_ROOT env). - Record child PID on WorkerRecord; ensure watchdog SIGTERM/SIGKILL via PID still works. - Bridge child-emitted progress events into the existing parent SSE subscriber path — either by POST to /api/workers/{id}/event being multiplexed back to the in-memory progressCh, or by tailing the child's worker-events.jsonl. - Stop()/cancel/wake plumbing: Stop signals the PID; wakeCh is replaced by sending a SIGUSR1 (or equivalent) handled by ddx work, OR documented as no-op when subprocess. - Drop in-process LandCoordinator coupling, in-process DefaultBeadReviewer, in-process CostCapTracker, in-process RoutePreflight from this code path. - Update workers_test.go, workers_costcap_test.go, workers_gates_test.go, workers_watchdog_test.go, workers_wake_test.go, workers_reap_unix.go, workers_stop_test.go, workers_stop_propagation_test.go, workers_e2e_test.go, graphql_subscription_worker_test.go to use a subprocess-stub binary (or test-binary reentry) instead of BeadWorkerFactory injection. - handleStartExecuteLoopWorker untouched at this point — still uses ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec. Acceptance: subprocess exec path works; existing test scenarios pass against subprocess-stub; lefthook pre-commit green. Estimated: ~800–1,000 LOC. ### 7b — handler migration + ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec deletion Title: ADR-022 step 7b: handleStartExecuteLoopWorker → exec ddx work; delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec Scope: - handleStartExecuteLoopWorker decodes HTTP request → builds argv/env → calls a thinner StartExecuteLoop(opts) that no longer takes the legacy ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec struct. - Delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec struct and its JSON marshalling. - Update server_test.go, bounded_egress_test.go, federation_*_test.go, and any other call sites that constructed an ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec literal. Acceptance: spec struct gone; handler tests assert exec form; full ./internal/server/... + ./cmd/... green. Estimated: ~300–500 LOC. ### 7c — End-to-end integration test (scripted-claude-stub or opt-in real claude) Title: ADR-022 step 7c: end-to-end test — server-spawned worker via exec path registers, invokes harness on fixture bead, lands commit Scope: Build the AC #5 evidence the parent bead requires: - Spin up a server.Server in-test bound to an ephemeral port. - Use scripts/build-fixture-repo.sh (federated profile) for a clean ddx-initialized git repo with one ready bead. - POST /api/agent/workers/execute-loop with --once. - Assert the child ddx work process registers via POST /api/workers/register, claims the bead, the scripted-claude-stub (matching the same exec args + env-var path real claude uses) emits an evidence file, the attempt commits, and the bead closes (or preserves with a known-good status). - Optional: gate a separate test on `DDX_E2E_REAL_CLAUDE=1` that runs the same scenario against a real `claude` binary on $PATH. Acceptance: green CI run with scripted-claude-stub end-to-end; opt-in real-claude variant documented. Estimated: ~400–600 LOC + fixture infra. ### Dependencies - 7b dep 7a (handler migration assumes runWorker already speaks exec) - 7c dep 7b (end-to-end via the new exec path requires the new handler shape) ## What blocked this attempt from a clean partial commit The 6 ACs are tightly interlocking — partial implementation (e.g. just runWorker rewrite without handler migration, or rewrite without test sweep) leaves the package in a non-compiling or red-test state (BeadWorkerFactory injection points either still exist and are dead, or are removed and break tests). A clean intermediate slice requires the explicit decomposition above so each child commits a green tree. ## Bookkeeping A child bead was inadvertently created from this worktree before the orchestrator-must-be-main-repo issue was identified; its ID `.execute-bead-wt-ddx-eccc6efb-20260503T065047-61e4ed20-c4de2d6e` is malformed and the bead has been closed. Six tightly-coupled ACs touch the most intricate file in the server package.\n\n### Actual surface (vs stated estimate)\n\ncli/internal/server/workers.go is 1,959 lines. The current `runWorker` (lines 612–926, ~315 lines) is a dense in-process orchestrator that knits together:\n\n- in-process `agent.ExecuteBeadWorker` construction\n- per-project `LandCoordinator` single-flight serialization\n- in-process post-merge `DefaultBeadReviewer`\n- in-process `escalation.CostCapTracker` (per-worker billed cost accumulation)\n- in-process `RoutePreflight` (typed-incompatibility gate via FizeauService)\n- per-attempt `singleTierAttempt` closure with config resolution + gate evaluation + leftover-evidence safety net\n- progress channel piped to SSE subscribers via `drainProgress`\n- watchdog phase-stall tracking via shared workerHandle.lastPhaseTS\n- terminal-state preservation (stopped/reaped) merged with loop result\n- BeadWorkerFactory injection point for test fakes\n\nReplacing the in-process call with `exec ddx work` collapses all of those in-process couplings — they now run inside the child process — but the parent server still needs equivalent observability/control via:\n\n- /api/workers/{id}/event ingestion (already exists from step 1) for progress\n- PID + signal escalation for watchdog/Stop (PID field exists; signal plumbing is partial)\n- worker-events.jsonl tail or HTTP-mirrored events for SSE bridging\n- Cross-process Stop / wakeCh equivalent\n\n### Test surface\n\ncli/internal/server/workers_test.go alone is 1,107 lines, plus 9 sibling workers_*_test.go files and graphql_subscription_worker_test.go that all assert in-process semantics. Many tests inject `BeadWorkerFactory` for control over the in-process worker; with subprocess execution, those tests need to be replaced by either:\n\n- a built-in subprocess-stub binary (e.g. `go test -test.run … -ddx-fake-work-stub` reentry pattern) the parent execs in tests, or\n- deletion as no-longer-applicable (semantics now belong to ddx work's own test surface).\n\nThis is the bulk of the work, not \"test updates.\"\n\n### AC #5 (real-harness end-to-end)\n\n\"server-spawned worker (via the new exec path) registers via the new endpoints AND successfully invokes the claude harness (real claude binary, not a stub) on a fixture bead AND completes the attempt with a commit\"\n\nEven with the fall-back to a scripted-claude-stub, this requires: a clean fixture repo with a real ready bead, a stub or real claude binary on $PATH inside the test harness, a server bound to a free port, the new exec path wired in, the worker registering against the running server's /api/workers/register, and an end-to-end commit landing through the LandCoordinator (which has moved out of the parent process). That is a substantial new integration test — comparable to the workers_e2e_test.go suite — distinct from the refactor proper.\n\n### Size-check verdict\n\n- More than ~6 ACs across unrelated subsystems? 6 ACs, but several (in-process-replacement refactor, struct deletion, full test sweep, real-harness integration) are independently sized engineering deliverables.\n- AC mixes implementation, integration tests, and end-to-end scenarios? Yes (AC #1–4 = refactor, AC #5 = new integration test, AC #6 = full lefthook gate).\n- More than ~500 LOC across more than ~5 files? Yes — workers.go rewrite (~400), server.go handler (~70), workers_test.go sweep (~600+), sibling test sweep (~500+), new exec/stub plumbing (~200), new e2e integration test (~300). Estimated 1,500–2,500 LOC of changes.\n\nThe bead's \"~200 LOC + test updates\" estimate (echoing the ADR-022 step 7 roadmap line) materially understates the work.\n\n## Proposed decomposition (orchestrator should create from main repo)\n\nCreate the following children of ddx-eccc6efb from the **main repo** (not from an execution worktree — IDs created from a worktree get the worktree dirname as the project prefix and become unusable):\n\n### 7a — runWorker exec rewrite + parent-side subprocess plumbing\nTitle: ADR-022 step 7a: workers.go runWorker → exec ddx work subprocess (progress via /api/workers/{id}/event, watchdog via PID)\nScope:\n- Replace the in-process `worker.Run(...)` body in cli/internal/server/workers.go with `exec.CommandContext` of `ddx work` (resolved via os.Executable() + arg-vec flags + DDX_PROJECT_ROOT env).\n- Record child PID on WorkerRecord; ensure watchdog SIGTERM/SIGKILL via PID still works.\n- Bridge child-emitted progress events into the existing parent SSE subscriber path — either by POST to /api/workers/{id}/event being multiplexed back to the in-memory progressCh, or by tailing the child's worker-events.jsonl.\n- Stop()/cancel/wake plumbing: Stop signals the PID; wakeCh is replaced by sending a SIGUSR1 (or equivalent) handled by ddx work, OR documented as no-op when subprocess.\n- Drop in-process LandCoordinator coupling, in-process DefaultBeadReviewer, in-process CostCapTracker, in-process RoutePreflight from this code path.\n- Update workers_test.go, workers_costcap_test.go, workers_gates_test.go, workers_watchdog_test.go, workers_wake_test.go, workers_reap_unix.go, workers_stop_test.go, workers_stop_propagation_test.go, workers_e2e_test.go, graphql_subscription_worker_test.go to use a subprocess-stub binary (or test-binary reentry) instead of BeadWorkerFactory injection.\n- handleStartExecuteLoopWorker untouched at this point — still uses ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec.\nAcceptance: subprocess exec path works; .ddx/beads.jsonl:1107:{"acceptance":"1. max_billable_usd enforced per session: turn rejected with COST_CAP_EXCEEDED when cumulative cost_usd would exceed limit.\n2. Reviewer routing consults Story 17 preferred_reviewer first, then Story 10 escalation ladder.\n3. Downgrade routing: unavailable preferred reviewer falls back to next ladder rung with review-pairing-degraded event.\n4. Three refusal codes implemented: PROMPT_BUDGET_EXCEEDED, COST_CAP_EXCEEDED, REVIEWER_UNAVAILABLE.\n5. TestReview_CostCap_ReturnsCostCapExceeded verifies enforcement at max_billable_usd.\n6. TestReview_ReviewerUnavailable_Downgrades verifies downgrade path and event.\n7. TestReview_RefusalCodes_HaveStructuredBody verifies all three codes return JSON body.\n8. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/server/... green.\n9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-05-02T07:06:56.655171593Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-f1e12904","depends_on_id":"ddx-0e5c3005","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-02T07:06:56Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM\nReview sessions have no cost cap enforcement, no reviewer routing policy, and no structured refusal contracts. Without these, a review session can run unbounded cost, use an unavailable reviewer, or fail with an unstructured error that the frontend cannot surface to the operator.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- cli/internal/server/review_session.go (from ddx-e7b80a50, a dep): ReviewSession has max_billable_usd field but no enforcement code.\n- No reviewer routing logic exists in the server review path — preferred_reviewer from Story 17 and the escalation ladder from Story 10 are not consulted.\n- No structured refusal error codes exist (PROMPT_BUDGET_EXCEEDED, COST_CAP_EXCEEDED, REVIEWER_UNAVAILABLE).\n- ddx-0e5c3005 (reviewer routing contract, a dep) must land first.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Enforce max_billable_usd per session: before each turn, check cumulative cost_usd against max_billable_usd; if exceeded, return COST_CAP_EXCEEDED refusal.\n- Reviewer routing: consult Story 17's preferred_reviewer first; fall back to Story 10's escalation ladder.\n- Downgrade routing: when preferred reviewer is unavailable, downgrade to next ladder rung and emit review-pairing-degraded event.\n- Implement three structured refusal error codes as typed errors with structured JSON body.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Turn dispatcher implementation (ddx-f7c3d512, a dep).\n- Frontend display of refusal codes (handled by review panel bead).","id":"ddx-f1e12904","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","story:18","area:server","kind:feature"],"parent":"ddx-42b917fe","priority":2,"status":"open","title":"review: cost cap + reviewer routing/downgrade + refusal contracts","updated_at":"2026-05-04T16:25:43.34277059Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:1130:{"acceptance":"(1) A regression test at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_evidence_test.go (or execute_bead_land_test.go) runs a simulated successful execute-bead attempt against a real temp git repo and asserts: (a) after the run returns, the working tree is clean (git status --porcelain is empty), and (b) at least one file under .ddx/executions/\u003cattempt-id\u003e/ is present in git log --all --oneline for the attempt's result or successor commit. Test must fail on current HEAD (98e379b) and pass post-fix. (2) The same test uses bead tracker evidence to confirm the bead's closing_commit_sha still resolves after the merge (git cat-file -e). (3) cli/internal/server/workers_test.go TestWorkerManagerStartAndShow (or a sibling test) exercises the server-worker path and asserts the same clean-worktree guarantee. (4) Full test suite (cli: go test ./cmd ./internal/agent ./internal/server) passes. (5) Manual dogfooding: ddx agent execute-loop --once against a ready bead leaves git status --porcelain empty, and HEAD or HEAD^ contains at least one file under .ddx/executions/\u003cattempt\u003e/. (6) No changes to AGENTS.md, FEAT-006, or skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md — the policy is already correct; this is implementation only.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"ce25dda3c76977d7d66e0a39dc6e93b0f2b25183","created_at":"2026-04-15T15:34:35.535203989Z","description":"## Scope\n\nexecute-bead's orchestrator currently leaves the per-attempt evidence files it\nmaterializes (`.ddx/executions/\u003cattempt-id\u003e/manifest.json`, `prompt.md`,\n`result.json`, `checks.json`, `usage.json`) untracked after the agent run\nreturns. Successful bead commits reach main via the \"Merge bead\" path, but the\nevidence sits in the working tree unstaged and orphaned. This bead closes that\ngap: after the agent finishes, DDx (not the agent) stages the evidence and\nreturns the working tree clean.\n\nConcrete evidence of the gap: commit 98e379b on main is a one-shot backfill\nthat imports 254 historical `.ddx/executions/\u003cts\u003e-\u003chash\u003e/` directories\n(2026-04-11 → 2026-04-15) that were never staged by the execute-bead pipeline.\nThat backfill must not need to happen again.\n\n## Files to Touch\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go` — the land/merge flow. After the\n  agent's commit is produced (or after `Merge()` / bare-ref update), and\n  before returning `ExecuteBeadReport`, stage the per-attempt evidence dir and\n  fold it into the merge. Two shapes are acceptable:\n  (a) amend the evidence into the bead's own commit (agent's commit + DDx's\n      evidence files, single commit), OR\n  (b) emit a trailing `chore: add execution evidence [\u003cattempt-id\u003e]` commit\n      on the working branch as a sibling to the bead's commit, in the same\n      merge transaction.\n  Choose (b) if amending the agent's commit would rewrite its SHA — the agent\n  commit's SHA is referenced by `session_id` / `closing_commit_sha` and must\n  not move. Choose (a) only if the evidence can be staged BEFORE the agent\n  commits (e.g. the agent is told via `git add` hooks to pick up a DDx-seeded\n  index). Pragmatic recommendation: (b).\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` — the per-run orchestrator entry. After\n  the executor returns, verify the working tree is clean; if it is not,\n  diagnose untracked files introduced by this run, stage them into the\n  trailing evidence commit (if they are under `.ddx/executions/\u003cattempt\u003e/`),\n  and fail the run with a clear error otherwise (\"orchestrator left worktree\n  dirty: \u003cpath\u003e\").\n- `cli/internal/server/workers.go` — the server-worker path (`runWorker`,\n  `buildAgentRunner`). Same contract applies: after `ExecuteBead` returns and\n  `LandBeadResult` is called, the worker must verify the worktree is clean\n  before declaring the attempt complete. If the land flow is still dropping\n  evidence, surface that as a test failure, not a silent leak.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go` OR a new\n  `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_evidence_test.go` — regression tests.\n\n## Files NOT to Touch\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` lines 875-960 — the per-bead\n  instructions text (`executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText` /\n  `executeBeadInstructionsAgentText`). Those are agent-facing and must keep\n  telling the agent to `git add \u003cspecific-paths\u003e` only. The fix belongs in\n  the orchestrator, not the prompt: DDx wrote the evidence, DDx commits it.\n- `AGENTS.md`, `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md`,\n  `skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md` — the policy is already correct as of commit\n  3a2a0c5. This bead implements the policy; it does not amend it.\n\n## Cross-Bead Context\n\n- Parent epic `ddx-32b3008e` (\"Evolve execute-bead into a tracked\n  execution-evidence workflow\", P0, spec-id FEAT-006) is the meta for this\n  work. This bead is the first execution-layer task under that epic.\n- The trail-preservation policy is codified in commit 3a2a0c5: AGENTS.md\n  \"Merge Policy\" section, FEAT-006 \"Execution Trail Preservation in\n  Downstream Merges\" section (with 3 acceptance criteria), and\n  skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md \"Branch Merge Policy\" section.\n- The land flow today uses a mix of `git merge --no-ff` and\n  `git update-ref` (for bare-repo worktrees) — see\n  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:166-544 for the three code paths\n  that touch the merge. Any of them may be the right insertion point; pick\n  the one that runs LAST after the agent commit is finalized.\n- The bug-fix commit 2792dfc (\"anchor SessionLogDir to project root\") gives\n  the orchestrator a reliable absolute path for session logs. This bead is\n  the analogous fix for execution evidence.\n\n## Spec Excerpt (FEAT-006 Execution Trail Preservation, normative)\n\n\u003e **Execution Trail Preservation in Downstream Merges** [...]\n\u003e Bead records store `closing_commit_sha` as a pointer back into git\n\u003e history; routing, cost, latency, retry-count, and tier-escalation reports\n\u003e read these commits directly. The trail realizes the `output = bead(input)`\n\u003e contract the system is built on: every bead attempt is recoverable from\n\u003e git history alone.\n\u003e\n\u003e **Acceptance Criteria (execution trail preservation):**\n\u003e - Given a branch containing at least one `chore: update tracker\n\u003e   (execute-bead` commit, when the branch is merged to its target, then\n\u003e   every commit SHA on the feature branch remains reachable from the target\n\u003e   branch HEAD after the merge.\n\u003e - Given a bead has `closing_commit_sha` set, when the branch containing\n\u003e   that commit is merged to main, then `git cat-file -e\n\u003e   \u003cclosing_commit_sha\u003e` still succeeds on main after the merge.\n\nThis bead adds a fourth, implementation-layer criterion: that the\nper-attempt evidence dir is *present in the trail at all*, not merely\npreserved across merges.\n\n## Verification Commands\n\n- `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent -run TestExecuteBeadLandsEvidence`\n  passes (new regression test, see AC below).\n- `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent ./internal/server ./cmd` passes\n  (full suite; no regressions).\n- Manual dogfooding: in a throwaway clone, run\n  `ddx agent execute-loop --once` against a ready bead. After it returns,\n  `git status --porcelain` MUST print nothing. `git log -1 --stat` MUST show\n  at least one file under `.ddx/executions/\u003cattempt\u003e/` in the HEAD commit\n  (or its immediate predecessor in the same merge).","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"resolved_provider\":\"agent\",\"route_reason\":\"first-available\",\"fallback_chain\":[],\"base_url\":\"http://vidar:1234/v1\"}","created_at":"2026-04-16T04:47:09.142456953Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=agent reason=first-available"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"resolved_provider\":\"agent\",\"route_reason\":\"first-available\",\"fallback_chain\":[],\"base_url\":\"http://bragi:1234/v1\"}","created_at":"2026-04-16T18:11:14.003181123Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=agent reason=first-available"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"resolved_provider\":\"claude\",\"resolved_model\":\"claude-opus-4-6\",\"fallback_chain\":[]}","created_at":"2026-04-16T18:34:21.429059193Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=claude model=claude-opus-4-6"}],"id":"ddx-f7ac7826","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","kind:implementation","area:agent","area:exec","area:git"],"parent":"ddx-32b3008e","priority":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-006","status":"closed","title":"orchestrator: land execution evidence and leave worktree clean after agent run","updated_at":"2026-04-16T18:34:37.770774687Z"}

The drain-queue failure mode (empty spec → gemini+minimax → 19-burn) is impossible.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **Upstream prerequisite tagged and pulled.** Go.mod references a ddx-agent version whose CHANGELOG documents: first-class `default` and `local` profiles; supported-models allow-list in routing inputs + gating; `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible`; public decision-trace (`RouteDecision.Candidates` + typed error); cost dimension on `ProviderEntry` with unknown-cost fallback. All upstream beads in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/` labeled `routing-plan` are closed.\n\n2. **No DDx-side synthesis on default path.**\n   - `ddx agent run` with zero flags issues exactly one `ResolveRoute(Profile: \"default\")` call, no Model, no Harness, no Provider.\n   - `ddx work` default: same shape.\n   - `ddx agent execute-bead` with no overrides: same shape.\n   - Integration test: same config that caused the drain-queue failure (real `~/.local/share/ddx/.ddx/config.yaml`) now either succeeds or returns a single typed error — never 19-burn.\n   - Unit test: `workerDispatchAdapter.DispatchWorker({})` → spec with `Profile: \"default\"`, no other fields. No `ResolveTierModelRef`, no `ResolveProfileLadder` in the call graph on the default path.\n\n3. **Preflight before claim.**\n   - Call sequence at `execute_bead_loop.go:352`: next-candidate → `ResolveRoute` → (preflight gate, new) → `Claim` → invoke. Route error OR preflight rejection exits the loop with a `worker-level` failure — no bead claimed.\n   - Test: seed a pathological route (harness with allow-list that excludes the decision's model — fabricated via upstream test seam). Assert `beadStore.Claim` is never called; worker record shows `status: execution_failed` with detail naming the rejected pair; no bead has a `kind:tier-attempt` event from this attempt.\n\n4. **Config deprecation is deliberate, not warning-only.**\n   - Loading a config with `agent.routing.default_harness` set produces a **hard error** at startup with a migration pointer. Field is deleted from the config schema.\n   - Loading a config with `agent.routing.profile_ladders` or `agent.routing.model_overrides` set produces a one-time warning per process AND causes the default path to ignore them. Opt-in with `--escalate` or `--override-model` explicit flags.\n   - CHANGELOG entry + migration guide in DDx docs.\n   - Migration test: fixture config containing all three deprecated fields, run `ddx agent run` with no flags, assert warnings printed and default path honored (not the deprecated config).\n\n5. **Override behavior matches G3 exactly.**\n   - `--harness claude` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: \"default\", Harness: \"claude\")`. If claude fails upstream's gates (binary missing, quota exhausted), surfaces the typed error; no silent substitution. E2E test mocks each gate failure.\n   - `--model opus-4.7` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: \"default\", Model: \"opus-4.7\")`. Lowest-cost available quota-OK provider wins. E2E test seeds 3 providers at different cost with shared model; asserts lowest-cost pick.\n   - `--harness claude --model opus-4.7` → typed `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` when combo is invalid; clean dispatch otherwise.\n   - `--profile local --harness claude` → upstream typed error (\"claude is non-local, profile=local is local-only\"); DDx surfaces it unmodified. E2E test.\n\n6. **Playwright — drain-queue goldens.**\n   - Fixture matches user's real config (endpoints: lmstudio + omlx + lmstudio + lmstudio, no gemini binary test or with gemini installed but not in allow-list for the chosen model).\n   - Click Drain on project home → within 5s: worker row appears with harness = `agent` (or another live-endpoint-backed harness). Never gemini + non-gemini model. Never 19 failures.\n   - After drain completes: worker status `success` (or typed error exit with reason recorded on the bead). Attempts count matches actual work done.\n\n7. **No compensating DDx logic retained.**\n   - Search DDx for code that re-implements any of: allow-list checks, exact-pin filtering, profile-to-preference mapping, provider cost scoring. Must be absent. If found, PR fails review.\n   - `ResolveProfileLadder`, `ResolveTierModelRef`, `workersByHarness` escalation helpers are deleted from the default path. They remain reachable via `--escalate` only. Dead-code detector run in CI catches orphans.\n\n8. **D6 / D7 are explicitly out of scope and filed as follow-ups.**\n   - `ddx-05b4cc9d` (workers filter) remains its own bead and may land before or after this one.\n   - A new bead is filed post-release for the route-status / providers-UI decision-trace consumer work, pointing at A4 upstream as satisfied.\n\n9. **Documentation.**\n   - DDx's CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md links to upstream profile catalog + override precedence + best-provider contract docs. No re-documentation on the DDx side.\n   - DDx CHANGELOG entry covers the go.mod bump, D4 breaking changes, and new CLI surface semantics.\n\n10. **Regression goldens for the drain-queue bug.**\n    - Integration test exists that reproduces the original failure mode (empty spec + user's config + gemini binary installed + minimax model in overrides). Before this bead's work: test fails with 19-burn. After: test succeeds or exits with one clean error.
