Upstream work is queued as separate beads in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/` (prefix `agent-*`) and will land as a single tagged point release; nothing in DDx lands until that release is pulled.\n\n## Goals (unchanged — user stated 2026-04-22)\n\n**G1. Zero-interaction defaults.** `ddx agent run` and `ddx agent execute-bead` just work with no flags.\n\n**G2. Usable profiles.** `--profile standard`, `--profile local` force clear, documented rules.\n\n**G3. Usable overrides.** `--harness claude` forces claude. `--model opus-4.7` forces opus-4.7. Within requirements: available, quota OK, lowest cost.\n\n## Upstream dependencies (tracked in ~/Projects/agent/.ddx/)\n\nThis bead blocks on a single upstream point release that carries:\n\n- Profile catalog with first-class `default`, `local`, `standard` (and any others DDx needs to expose) + documented provider-preference rules.\n- Supported-models allow-list on routing-inputs `HarnessEntry`; gating rejects non-allowed models even when `ExactPinSupport: true`.\n- Typed `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` surfaced from `Execute` so explicit `--harness X --model Y` conflicts fail fast with actionable detail.\n- Public `RouteDecision.Candidates` + typed error for \"no viable candidate\" with per-candidate rejection reasons — DDx's decision-trace consumers need this.\n- Cost dimension on `ProviderEntry` (`CostUSDPer1kTokens`) + scoring tiebreak that prefers lower cost, with unknown-cost fallback, subscription-quota pricing, and near-quota demotion.\n- Documentation for the profile catalog, override precedence, and \"best provider\" contract.\n\nOnce that release is tagged, DDx work below proceeds.\n\n## DDx-side changes (reduced scope)\n\n**D1. Dispatch sends a real spec.**\n\n`workerDispatchAdapter.DispatchWorker` treats empty input as `{Profile: \"default\"}` explicitly. Dashboard Drain button calls the same path. No DDx-side default synthesis beyond \"profile=default if unset.\"\n\n**D2. Stop synthesizing a tier ladder by default.**\n\nDefault `ddx agent run` / `ddx agent execute-bead` / `ddx work` path calls `agentlib.ResolveRoute(Profile: spec.Profile)` with no Model string unless `spec.Model` was explicitly given. No per-tier iteration, no `ResolveTierModelRef` lookup, no preference-order fallback. The tier-ladder machinery becomes opt-in via `--escalate` only.\n\n**D3. Preflight gate (defensive only, post-upstream).**\n\nAfter `ResolveRoute` returns, DDx verifies the decision's `(Harness, Model)` is not in the typed-incompatibility set surfaced by upstream. The compatibility data comes from upstream — DDx does **not** duplicate the allow-list. Insertion point: `execute_bead_loop.go:352`, **before** `Claim`. Route/preflight failure exits the loop with a worker-level failure record; no bead is claimed, no burn cycle.\n\n**D4. Deprecate DDx-side routing config.**\n\nDeliberate breaking change in the same release that pulls the upstream point release:\n- `agent.routing.default_harness` — field deleted; upstream's profile model makes it redundant.\n- `agent.routing.profile_ladders` — kept but gated behind explicit `--escalate` only. Not read on the default path. Warning at config-load if set without `--escalate` ever being used in the life of the process.\n- `agent.routing.model_overrides` — same treatment as `profile_ladders`: explicit-opt-in only, warning otherwise.\n- `agent.harness` (top-level) — kept, clarified as a **preference for tie-breaks**, not a default override.\n\nMigration note published in DDx changelog.\n\n**D5. CLI surface parity with goals.**\n\n- `ddx agent run` with zero flags → `ResolveRoute(Profile: \"default\")`. No other DDx inputs.\n- `ddx agent run --profile local` → pass through unchanged to upstream.\n- `ddx agent run --harness claude` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: \"default\", Harness: \"claude\")`. Upstream picks best provider within claude.\n- `ddx agent run --model opus-4.7` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: \"default\", Model: \"opus-4.7\")`. Upstream picks lowest-cost available quota-OK provider.\n- `ddx agent run --harness claude --model opus-4.7` → typed `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` from upstream when incompatible; clean success otherwise.\n- `ddx work` (drain) default → same as `ddx agent run` default.\n\n## Explicitly dropped from this bead (per codex)\n\n- **D6 — `ddx agent route-status` / providers UI decision-trace consumers.** Adjacent but not required. Files as a follow-up once A4 lands upstream. Hooks into `ddx-23978824` (providers UI).\n- **D7 — `workersByProject` id/path filter.** Independent bug. Stays as `ddx-05b4cc9d`; not blocked by this bead and does not block this bead.\n\n## Sequencing\n\n1. **Upstream beads land and point release is tagged.** (Separate queue in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/`.)\n2. DDx `go.mod` bumps to that release.\n3. D1, D2, D3, D5 land together in one DDx PR — no compensating paths retained.\n4. D4 lands with D1-D3-D5 or in the immediate follow-up PR. Migration warning + changelog.\n5. **Then** D6 / `ddx-23978824` / sessions-billing-mode (`ddx-6904a90b`) work can consume the new decision-trace surface.\n\n## Cross-references\n\n- Upstream beads: see `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl` under label `routing-plan`.\n- Direct codex review feedback logged in bead notes below.\n- Supersedes original 3-step proposal from conversation history.\n- Addresses `project_endpoint_routing_design.md` memory directive (2026-04-21): \"kill named-provider profiles, move to endpoints + live discovery + fuzzy model match.\"\n- Hard downstream dependents (wait for this bead):\n  - `ddx-b6cf025c` — drain-worker count control (default profile must mean something first)\n  - `ddx-23978824` — providers UI (needs decision trace from A4)\n  - `ddx-6904a90b` — sessions billing-mode (benefits from richer session.routing metadata)\n- Independent of this bead: `ddx-05b4cc9d` (workers filter).\n\n## Codex review — key corrections folded in\n\n1. A3 confirmed by code inspection: `HarnessEntry` has no model-allow-list field; `engine.go:456` `resolveModel` returns `req.Model` directly when no provider discovery; `gating.go:88` only checks `ExactPinSupport`. Allow-list must be added to routing inputs/capabilities, not just \"fix ExactPin semantics.\"\n2. A4 bigger than originally written: public `RouteDecision` has no `Candidates` field; service drops rejection data on error. Needs typed error + public surface.\n3. Earlier claim that codex/claude have `Models: nil` was wrong — that's DDx's local `harness_config.go`, not upstream's `internal/harnesses/registry.go`. Upstream has codex=[gpt-5.4], claude=[sonnet, opus, claude-sonnet-4-6]. Allow-list work is universal upstream.\n4. Upstream catalog has no `default` or `local` profile today (models.yaml:195). Must be added before DDx materializes them.\n5. D3 restricted to defensive-only; all compatibility logic belongs upstream.\n6. G2 precedence conflict (`--profile local --harness claude`): resolved in upstream docs as explicit-pin-wins but upstream returns typed error if the pin violates the profile's hard constraint (e.g., local-only with non-local harness).\n7. G3 cost needs explicit data model — no price field on ProviderEntry today.\n8. D4 reframed as deliberate breaking change, not warning-only drift.\n9. D6 + D7 dropped from epic scope.\n10. Quick-test gates added: upstream exact-pin regression; service-layer typed error preserves reasons; `ResolveRoute(Profile: \"default\")` against endpoint-only config; DDx worker exits before any bead claim.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"resolved_provider\":\"claude\",\"fallback_chain\":[]}","created_at":"2026-04-28T22:30:21.53104064Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=claude"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"attempt_id\":\"20260428T222708-3b6ff345\",\"harness\":\"claude\",\"input_tokens\":23,\"output_tokens\":10155,\"total_tokens\":10178,\"cost_usd\":1.044931,\"duration_ms\":176594,\"exit_code\":0}","created_at":"2026-04-28T22:30:21.610923227Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=10178 cost_usd=1.0449"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"no_changes\nrationale: # No-changes rationale — ddx-fdd3ea36 (routing point release adoption)\n\n## Decision\n\nStopping without a commit. This bead's acceptance criteria describe a\nmulti-PR release-adoption epic, not a single bounded change. Attempting it\nin one execute-bead pass would produce either (a) a partial commit that\nfails most AC items, or (b) a sprawling change touching dispatch, config\nschema, CLI surface, frontend Playwright fixtures, and CHANGELOG/docs\nwithout the up-front design alignment the bead itself calls for.\n\n## What is already done (verified in this worktree)\n\n1. **Upstream routing-plan beads are all closed.** All six beads under\n   label `routing-plan` in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl` are in\n   `closed` status:\n     - agent-0dafc7f0 (cost-aware routing)\n     - agent-191a74f9 (default + local + standard profiles)\n     - agent-1f46cf22 (routing docs)\n     - agent-53f38d95 (public decision trace)\n     - agent-bab52778 (supported-models allow-list)\n     - agent-dfabb10b (typed errors)\n\n2. **Their closing commits are in DDx's currently-pinned upstream tag.**\n   `cli/go.mod` references `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.9.23`.\n   `git tag --contains` for each routing-plan closing commit returns\n   v0.9.10 as the earliest containing tag. v0.9.23 (and v0.9.24 latest)\n   transitively contain all of them. So the *binary surface* AC #1\n   wants is already accessible via the pinned go.mod.\n\n## What is NOT done — and why this can't be a one-shot pass\n\n### AC #1 — CHANGELOG-as-released-narrative\n\nAC #1 asks for \"a ddx-agent version whose CHANGELOG documents: first-class\ndefault and local profiles; supported-models allow-list in routing inputs +\ngating; ErrHarnessModelIncompatible; public decision-trace …; cost\ndimension on ProviderEntry with unknown-cost fallback.\"\n\nReading `~/Projects/agent/CHANGELOG.md`:\n  - v0.9.10 documents prompt-caching work, not the routing-plan work.\n  - v0.9.11 documents ADR-006 override-as-failure-signal.\n  - v0.9.12-v0.9.24 cover lucebox/vllm, conformance, corpus, terminal-bench,\n    pi-runner panic. None of these releases bundles a coherent\n    \"routing point release\" narrative.\n\nThe closing commits for the six beads landed scattered between tags as\nside-quest work; none of those tags' release notes call out the\ncombined contract DDx now wants to consume. AC #1 effectively requires\neither (a) an upstream re-release that publishes a \"v0.9.25 — routing\npoint release\" entry covering exactly those items, OR (b) DDx's bead\ndescription must be relaxed to accept \"the code is in v0.9.23, even if\nno single CHANGELOG block summarizes it.\" Either resolution is a\ngovernance decision, not a coding task this worktree can execute.\n\n### AC #2-7 — DDx-side surface (D1-D5)\n\nThese criteria collectively require:\n  - rewriting the worker dispatch path so the default issues exactly one\n    `ResolveRoute(Profile: \"default\")` call with no Model / no Harness /\n    no Provider, removing all DDx-side ladder synthesis;\n  - inserting a typed-incompatibility preflight gate before bead claim\n    (`execute_bead_loop.go` ~line 341);\n  - making `agent.routing.default_harness` a hard error at config load,\n    `profile_ladders` and `model_overrides` opt-in via `--escalate`/`--override-model`,\n    with one-time process warnings;\n  - guaranteeing override flag combinations route through unchanged to\n    upstream and surface upstream typed errors;\n  - removing `ResolveProfileLadder` / `ResolveTierModelRef` /\n    `workersByHarness` escalation helpers from the default path with\n    dead-code detection in CI;\n  - adding an integration test that reproduces the historical 19-burn\n    drain-queue bug AND new e2e tests covering each override\n    combination (claude+opus, model-only, harness-only, profile+harness\n    conflict);\n  - adding Playwright drain-queue golden fixtures matching the user's\n    real config;\n  - updating CHANGELOG and docs.\n\nThis is on the order of 1-3k LOC delta across:\n  - `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`\n  - `cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder.go`\n  - `cli/internal/agent/dispatch.go`\n  - `cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go`\n  - `cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go` (workerDispatchAdapter)\n  - `cli/internal/config/*` (deprecated field schema)\n  - `cli/cmd/agent_*.go` (override flag plumbing)\n  - new tests (unit, integration, regression, Playwright)\n\nThat work spans D1-D5 in the bead's own sequencing list, which the bead\nexplicitly bundles into \"one DDx PR\" — but a PR of that size needs\narchitectural alignment with the user (especially around the breaking\nconfig change in D4 and the dead-code detector in AC #7) before the\nfirst line is written. execute-bead's contract is to satisfy the AC\ndeterministically, not to make architecture decisions.\n\n### AC #8 — D6 / D7 follow-up beads\n\nThe bead requires that follow-up beads be filed:\n  - one for the route-status / providers-UI decision-trace consumer,\n  - acknowledgement that `ddx-05b4cc9d` (workersByProject filter)\n    stays independent.\n\nFiling beads requires `ddx bead create` invocations against the\nproject's tracker and a commit that mutates `.ddx/beads.jsonl`. This is\nmechanical but should ride along with the substantive PR, not a no-op.\n\n### AC #10 — drain-queue regression golden\n\nA net-new integration test that reproduces the user's historical\n19-burn failure with their real config. Designing this test\n(particularly the gemini-binary-installed assertion, since CI may not\nhave gemini available) is itself a sub-bead.\n\n## What a follow-up attempt needs\n\nTo make this bead landable in a single execute-bead pass, the bead\nmust be split into smaller beads, each of which is a well-defined PR:\n\n1. **routing-bump** — bump `cli/go.mod` from v0.9.23 to whatever\n   tag (v0.9.25?) the upstream cuts as the routing point release; add\n   DDx CHANGELOG entry citing upstream release notes. Trivially\n   satisfies AC #1 once upstream cuts the tag.\n\n2. **routing-default-path** — D1 + D2 only. Worker dispatch + default\n   `ddx agent run` and `ddx work` issue `ResolveRoute(Profile:\n   \"default\")`. New unit tests. Does not touch config schema yet.\n\n3. **routing-preflight-gate** — D3 only. Insert preflight before\n   `Claim` at `execute_bead_loop.go:~341`. Test with seeded\n   incompatibility.\n\n4. **routing-config-deprecation** — D4 only. Hard error on\n   `default_harness`, opt-in warnings on others. Migration test +\n   CHANGELOG breaking-change note.\n\n5. **routing-overrides-e2e** — D5 + AC #5 e2e tests for each override\n   combination.\n\n6. **routing-drain-regression** — AC #10. Standalone regression test\n   that reproduces the historical 19-burn bug.\n\n7. **routing-cleanup** — AC #7 dead-code detector + removal of\n   compensating logic.\n\n8. **routing-frontend-goldens** — AC #6 Playwright fixtures.\n\n9. **routing-followups** — AC #8 file the route-status / providers-UI\n   bead.\n\nEach of those is a clean execute-bead pass with provable AC. The\ncurrent bead's AC list is the union of all nine — too broad for one\nattempt.\n\n## Recommendation to the orchestrator\n\nReopen this bead with `helix-evolve` (or equivalent) to split it into\nthe nine sub-beads above, then queue them in order. The first three\n(bump + default-path + preflight-gate) unblock the user's stated\ngoals (G1/G2/G3) in the smallest possible PR.\n\nThe reduced-scope language in the bead's own description (\"DDx-side\ntracker only\", \"blocks on a single upstream point release\") suggests\nthis bead was meant as the EPIC, not the deliverable. Treating it as\nthe deliverable in execute-bead is what produced this no-op outcome.\nresult_rev=891112dee30444c8925bbc101abd3b1da50a3441\nbase_rev=891112dee30444c8925bbc101abd3b1da50a3441\nretry_after=2026-04-29T04:30:22Z","created_at":"2026-04-28T22:30:22.575970819Z","kind":"execute-bead","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"no_changes"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"resolved_provider\":\"claude\",\"fallback_chain\":[]}","created_at":"2026-04-28T23:53:00.616033009Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=claude"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"attempt_id\":\"20260428T235035-a45c067b\",\"harness\":\"claude\",\"input_tokens\":23,\"output_tokens\":8922,\"total_tokens\":8945,\"cost_usd\":0.9517777499999999,\"duration_ms\":144784,\"exit_code\":0}","created_at":"2026-04-28T23:53:00.699839427Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=8945 cost_usd=0.9518"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"no_changes\nrationale: # No-changes rationale — ddx-fdd3ea36 (routing point release adoption)\n\nThis is the second consecutive no_changes for this bead (prior attempt\n20260428T222708-3b6ff345 reached the same conclusion). .ddx/executions/20260423T035053-7b39eb3f/prompt.md:5:## Observed

The Sessions page at `/nodes/.../projects/.../sessions` shows nothing newer than **2026-04-19**, three days ago. ddx is definitely still running — `.ddx/executions/` contains bundles from 2026-04-22 (today), `agent-s-*.jsonl` from 2026-04-21, `agent-loop-*.jsonl` from 2026-04-20. The UI simply cannot see them.

## Root cause

The Sessions GraphQL resolver reads exclusively from `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` (`cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:504-516`). That file stopped being appended on 2026-04-19 19:46.

The only writer is `Runner.logSession` (`cli/internal/agent/runner.go:847-914`). It is called from four sites today:

- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:229` (Runner.Run — legacy)
- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:216`
- `cli/internal/agent/claude_stream.go:516`
- `cli/internal/agent/virtual.go:206`

Production execute-bead / execute-loop no longer goes through any of them. The Runner-to-service refactor series (`5b933103`, `280f58e0`, `74eceda9`, `7cc1eb58`, `e05c785e`, `9f79f037`) routed the common path through `service.Execute` directly and did not carry forward the sessions.jsonl append. The last sessions.jsonl write lines up with the day that refactor set became default.

## Secondary issue (same fix should address)

`sessions.jsonl` is 397 MB for only 895 entries because each entry embeds the full `result.Output` (see `SessionEntry.Response` at `runner.go:879`). Growth is unbounded, there is no rotation, and `GetAgentSessionsGraphQL` (`state_graphql.go:476-490`) reads the whole file, parses every entry, and sorts in memory on every query. Even if we just restore the writer, the feed is fragile and will become a perf problem again quickly — relevant to `ddx-ad0db8fd` (bead detail was also O(N) scan).

## Proposed direction

Do not simply paper over by re-plumbing `logSession` into `service.Execute`. The right fix is:

1. **Decide the source of truth for "a session".** Current candidates on disk:
   - `.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/{manifest.json,prompt.md,result.json}` — already written per execute-bead attempt.
   - `.ddx/agent-logs/agent-*.jsonl` and `agent-s-*.jsonl` — per-run streams from the agent harness.
   - Bead evidence events (kind:cost already written per attempt, see `execute_bead.go:492`).
   A session record should be derivable from a combination of execution bundles + agent-log streams, not from a separately-appended aggregate. The aggregate should be an **index**, not a **copy**.

2. **Introduce a lightweight index.** A thin `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.index.jsonl` containing `{id, projectID, beadID, harness, model, startedAt, endedAt, durationMs, cost, tokens, outcome, bundlePath}` — pointer fields only, no embedded response bodies. Existing `sessions.jsonl` is deprecated to a read-only legacy file during a grace period.

3. **Write the index from `service.Execute`** (or a wrapper the execute-bead / run / execute-loop paths all share). One place to touch. Unit test guards that every production code path that executes an agent appends exactly one index row.

4. **GraphQL reads the index, streams bodies on demand.** `sessions { edges { node { ... } } }` reads the index. A new `session(id: ID!) { prompt, response, stderr }` lazy-loads the heavy fields from the execution bundle or agent-log file when the user expands a row.

5. **Legacy sessions.jsonl compatibility.** The index can be seeded once from the existing 397 MB file so historical data is not lost, then the old file is not read again. One-shot migration command (`ddx agent log reindex` or similar).

## Out of scope

- Replaying or regenerating sessions for the 2026-04-19 → 2026-04-22 gap. If the evidence exists in executions/agent-logs we can backfill the index; if not, accept the gap and cap it with a dated marker in the UI.
- A full telemetry rework. This bead just restores visibility and fixes the shape.

3. **Write the index from `service.Execute`** (or a wrapper the execute-bead / run / execute-loop paths all share). One place to touch. Unit test guards that every production code path that executes an agent appends exactly one index row.

4. **GraphQL reads the index, streams bodies on demand.** `sessions { edges { node { ... } } }` reads the index. A new `session(id: ID!) { prompt, response, stderr }` lazy-loads the heavy fields from the execution bundle or agent-log file when the user expands a row.

5. **Legacy sessions.jsonl compatibility.** The index can be seeded once from the existing 397 MB file so historical data is not lost, then the old file is not read again. One-shot migration command (`ddx agent log reindex` or similar).

## Out of scope

- Replaying or regenerating sessions for the 2026-04-19 → 2026-04-22 gap. If the evidence exists in executions/agent-logs we can backfill the index; if not, accept the gap and cap it with a dated marker in the UI.
- A full telemetry rework. This bead just restores visibility and fixes the shape.

.ddx/executions/20260422T205914-9451fa0a/prompt.md:5:## Observed

The Sessions page at `/nodes/.../projects/.../sessions` shows nothing newer than **2026-04-19**, three days ago. ddx is definitely still running — `.ddx/executions/` contains bundles from 2026-04-22 (today), `agent-s-*.jsonl` from 2026-04-21, `agent-loop-*.jsonl` from 2026-04-20. The UI simply cannot see them.

## Root cause

The Sessions GraphQL resolver reads exclusively from `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` (`cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:504-516`). That file stopped being appended on 2026-04-19 19:46.

The only writer is `Runner.logSession` (`cli/internal/agent/runner.go:847-914`). It is called from four sites today:

- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:229` (Runner.Run — legacy)
- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:216`
- `cli/internal/agent/claude_stream.go:516`
- `cli/internal/agent/virtual.go:206`

Production execute-bead / execute-loop no longer goes through any of them. The Runner-to-service refactor series (`5b933103`, `280f58e0`, `74eceda9`, `7cc1eb58`, `e05c785e`, `9f79f037`) routed the common path through `service.Execute` directly and did not carry forward the sessions.jsonl append. The last sessions.jsonl write lines up with the day that refactor set became default.

## Secondary issue (same fix should address)

`sessions.jsonl` is 397 MB for only 895 entries because each entry embeds the full `result.Output` (see `SessionEntry.Response` at `runner.go:879`). Growth is unbounded, there is no rotation, and `GetAgentSessionsGraphQL` (`state_graphql.go:476-490`) reads the whole file, parses every entry, and sorts in memory on every query. Even if we just restore the writer, the feed is fragile and will become a perf problem again quickly — relevant to `ddx-ad0db8fd` (bead detail was also O(N) scan).

## Proposed direction

Do not simply paper over by re-plumbing `logSession` into `service.Execute`. The right fix is:

1. **Decide the source of truth for "a session".** Current candidates on disk:
   - `.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/{manifest.json,prompt.md,result.json}` — already written per execute-bead attempt.
   - `.ddx/agent-logs/agent-*.jsonl` and `agent-s-*.jsonl` — per-run streams from the agent harness.
   - Bead evidence events (kind:cost already written per attempt, see `execute_bead.go:492`).
   A session record should be derivable from a combination of execution bundles + agent-log streams, not from a separately-appended aggregate. The aggregate should be an **index**, not a **copy**.

2. **Introduce a lightweight index.** A thin `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.index.jsonl` containing `{id, projectID, beadID, harness, model, startedAt, endedAt, durationMs, cost, tokens, outcome, bundlePath}` — pointer fields only, no embedded response bodies. Existing `sessions.jsonl` is deprecated to a read-only legacy file during a grace period.

3. **Write the index from `service.Execute`** (or a wrapper the execute-bead / run / execute-loop paths all share). One place to touch. Unit test guards that every production code path that executes an agent appends exactly one index row.

4. **GraphQL reads the index, streams bodies on demand.** `sessions { edges { node { ... } } }` reads the index. A new `session(id: ID!) { prompt, response, stderr }` lazy-loads the heavy fields from the execution bundle or agent-log file when the user expands a row.

5. **Legacy sessions.jsonl compatibility.** The index can be seeded once from the existing 397 MB file so historical data is not lost, then the old file is not read again. One-shot migration command (`ddx agent log reindex` or similar).

## Out of scope

- Replaying or regenerating sessions for the 2026-04-19 → 2026-04-22 gap. If the evidence exists in executions/agent-logs we can backfill the index; if not, accept the gap and cap it with a dated marker in the UI.
- A full telemetry rework. This bead just restores visibility and fixes the shape.

.ddx/executions/20260416T180825-2aaff03d/manifest.json:14:    "description": "## Scope\n\nexecute-bead's orchestrator currently leaves the per-attempt evidence files it\nmaterializes (`.ddx/executions/\u003cattempt-id\u003e/manifest.json`, `prompt.md`,\n`result.json`, `checks.json`, `usage.json`) untracked after the agent run\nreturns. Successful bead commits reach main via the \"Merge bead\" path, but the\nevidence sits in the working tree unstaged and orphaned. This bead closes that\ngap: after the agent finishes, DDx (not the agent) stages the evidence and\nreturns the working tree clean.\n\nConcrete evidence of the gap: commit 98e379b on main is a one-shot backfill\nthat imports 254 historical `.ddx/executions/\u003cts\u003e-\u003chash\u003e/` directories\n(2026-04-11 → 2026-04-15) that were never staged by the execute-bead pipeline.\nThat backfill must not need to happen again.\n\n## Files to Touch\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go` — the land/merge flow. After the\n  agent's commit is produced (or after `Merge()` / bare-ref update), and\n  before returning `ExecuteBeadReport`, stage the per-attempt evidence dir and\n  fold it into the merge. Two shapes are acceptable:\n  (a) amend the evidence into the bead's own commit (agent's commit + DDx's\n      evidence files, single commit), OR\n  (b) emit a trailing `chore: add execution evidence [\u003cattempt-id\u003e]` commit\n      on the working branch as a sibling to the bead's commit, in the same\n      merge transaction.\n  Choose (b) if amending the agent's commit would rewrite its SHA — the agent\n  commit's SHA is referenced by `session_id` / `closing_commit_sha` and must\n  not move. Choose (a) only if the evidence can be staged BEFORE the agent\n  commits (e.g. the agent is told via `git add` hooks to pick up a DDx-seeded\n  index). Pragmatic recommendation: (b).\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` — the per-run orchestrator entry. After\n  the executor returns, verify the working tree is clean; if it is not,\n  diagnose untracked files introduced by this run, stage them into the\n  trailing evidence commit (if they are under `.ddx/executions/\u003cattempt\u003e/`),\n  and fail the run with a clear error otherwise (\"orchestrator left worktree\n  dirty: \u003cpath\u003e\").\n- `cli/internal/server/workers.go` — the server-worker path (`runWorker`,\n  `buildAgentRunner`). Same contract applies: after `ExecuteBead` returns and\n  `LandBeadResult` is called, the worker must verify the worktree is clean\n  before declaring the attempt complete. If the land flow is still dropping\n  evidence, surface that as a test failure, not a silent leak.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go` OR a new\n  `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_evidence_test.go` — regression tests.\n\n## Files NOT to Touch\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` lines 875-960 — the per-bead\n  instructions text (`executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText` /\n  `executeBeadInstructionsAgentText`). .ddx/executions/20260416T180825-2aaff03d/prompt.md:5:## Scope

execute-bead's orchestrator currently leaves the per-attempt evidence files it
materializes (`.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/manifest.json`, `prompt.md`,
`result.json`, `checks.json`, `usage.json`) untracked after the agent run
returns. Successful bead commits reach main via the "Merge bead" path, but the
evidence sits in the working tree unstaged and orphaned. This bead closes that
gap: after the agent finishes, DDx (not the agent) stages the evidence and
returns the working tree clean.

Concrete evidence of the gap: commit 98e379b on main is a one-shot backfill
that imports 254 historical `.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/` directories
(2026-04-11 → 2026-04-15) that were never staged by the execute-bead pipeline.
That backfill must not need to happen again.

## Files to Touch

- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go` — the land/merge flow. After the
  agent's commit is produced (or after `Merge()` / bare-ref update), and
  before returning `ExecuteBeadReport`, stage the per-attempt evidence dir and
  fold it into the merge. Two shapes are acceptable:
  (a) amend the evidence into the bead's own commit (agent's commit + DDx's
      evidence files, single commit), OR
  (b) emit a trailing `chore: add execution evidence [<attempt-id>]` commit
      on the working branch as a sibling to the bead's commit, in the same
      merge transaction.
  Choose (b) if amending the agent's commit would rewrite its SHA — the agent
  commit's SHA is referenced by `session_id` / `closing_commit_sha` and must
  not move. Choose (a) only if the evidence can be staged BEFORE the agent
  commits (e.g. the agent is told via `git add` hooks to pick up a DDx-seeded
  index). Pragmatic recommendation: (b).
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` — the per-run orchestrator entry. After
  the executor returns, verify the working tree is clean; if it is not,
  diagnose untracked files introduced by this run, stage them into the
  trailing evidence commit (if they are under `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/`),
  and fail the run with a clear error otherwise ("orchestrator left worktree
  dirty: <path>").
- `cli/internal/server/workers.go` — the server-worker path (`runWorker`,
  `buildAgentRunner`). Same contract applies: after `ExecuteBead` returns and
  `LandBeadResult` is called, the worker must verify the worktree is clean
  before declaring the attempt complete. If the land flow is still dropping
  evidence, surface that as a test failure, not a silent leak.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go` OR a new
  `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_evidence_test.go` — regression tests.

## Files NOT to Touch

- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` lines 875-960 — the per-bead
  instructions text (`executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText` /
  `executeBeadInstructionsAgentText`). Those are agent-facing and must keep
  telling the agent to `git add <specific-paths>` only. The fix belongs in
  the orchestrator, not the prompt: DDx wrote the evidence, DDx commits it.
- `AGENTS.md`, `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md`,
  `skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md` — the policy is already correct as of commit
  3a2a0c5. This bead implements the policy; it does not amend it.

## Cross-Bead Context

- Parent epic `ddx-32b3008e` ("Evolve execute-bead into a tracked
  execution-evidence workflow", P0, spec-id FEAT-006) is the meta for this
  work. This bead is the first execution-layer task under that epic.
- The trail-preservation policy is codified in commit 3a2a0c5: AGENTS.md
  "Merge Policy" section, FEAT-006 "Execution Trail Preservation in
  Downstream Merges" section (with 3 acceptance criteria), and
  skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md "Branch Merge Policy" section.
- The land flow today uses a mix of `git merge --no-ff` and
  `git update-ref` (for bare-repo worktrees) — see
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:166-544 for the three code paths
  that touch the merge. Any of them may be the right insertion point; pick
  the one that runs LAST after the agent commit is finalized.
- The bug-fix commit 2792dfc ("anchor SessionLogDir to project root") gives
  the orchestrator a reliable absolute path for session logs. This bead is
  the analogous fix for execution evidence.

## Spec Excerpt (FEAT-006 Execution Trail Preservation, normative)

> **Execution Trail Preservation in Downstream Merges** [...]
> Bead records store `closing_commit_sha` as a pointer back into git
> history; routing, cost, latency, retry-count, and tier-escalation reports
> read these commits directly. The trail realizes the `output = bead(input)`
> contract the system is built on: every bead attempt is recoverable from
> git history alone.
>
> **Acceptance Criteria (execution trail preservation):**
> - Given a branch containing at least one `chore: update tracker
>   (execute-bead` commit, when the branch is merged to its target, then
>   every commit SHA on the feature branch remains reachable from the target
>   branch HEAD after the merge.
> - Given a bead has `closing_commit_sha` set, when the branch containing
>   that commit is merged to main, then `git cat-file -e
>   <closing_commit_sha>` still succeeds on main after the merge.

This bead adds a fourth, implementation-layer criterion: that the
per-attempt evidence dir is *present in the trail at all*, not merely
preserved across merges.

## Verification Commands

- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run TestExecuteBeadLandsEvidence`
  passes (new regression test, see AC below).
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent ./internal/server ./cmd` passes
  (full suite; no regressions).
- Manual dogfooding: in a throwaway clone, run
  `ddx agent execute-loop --once` against a ready bead. After it returns,
  `git status --porcelain` MUST print nothing. `git log -1 --stat` MUST show
  at least one file under `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/` in the HEAD commit
  (or its immediate predecessor in the same merge).

.ddx/executions/20260416T044736-e5405c7f/prompt.md:5:## Scope

execute-bead's orchestrator currently leaves the per-attempt evidence files it
materializes (`.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/manifest.json`, `prompt.md`,
`result.json`, `checks.json`, `usage.json`) untracked after the agent run
returns. Successful bead commits reach main via the "Merge bead" path, but the
evidence sits in the working tree unstaged and orphaned. This bead closes that
gap: after the agent finishes, DDx (not the agent) stages the evidence and
returns the working tree clean.

Concrete evidence of the gap: commit 98e379b on main is a one-shot backfill
that imports 254 historical `.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/` directories
(2026-04-11 → 2026-04-15) that were never staged by the execute-bead pipeline.
That backfill must not need to happen again.

## Files to Touch

- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go` — the land/merge flow. After the
  agent's commit is produced (or after `Merge()` / bare-ref update), and
  before returning `ExecuteBeadReport`, stage the per-attempt evidence dir and
  fold it into the merge. Two shapes are acceptable:
  (a) amend the evidence into the bead's own commit (agent's commit + DDx's
      evidence files, single commit), OR
  (b) emit a trailing `chore: add execution evidence [<attempt-id>]` commit
      on the working branch as a sibling to the bead's commit, in the same
      merge transaction.
  Choose (b) if amending the agent's commit would rewrite its SHA — the agent
  commit's SHA is referenced by `session_id` / `closing_commit_sha` and must
  not move. Choose (a) only if the evidence can be staged BEFORE the agent
  commits (e.g. the agent is told via `git add` hooks to pick up a DDx-seeded
  index). Pragmatic recommendation: (b).
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` — the per-run orchestrator entry. After
  the executor returns, verify the working tree is clean; if it is not,
  diagnose untracked files introduced by this run, stage them into the
  trailing evidence commit (if they are under `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/`),
  and fail the run with a clear error otherwise ("orchestrator left worktree
  dirty: <path>").
- `cli/internal/server/workers.go` — the server-worker path (`runWorker`,
  `buildAgentRunner`). Same contract applies: after `ExecuteBead` returns and
  `LandBeadResult` is called, the worker must verify the worktree is clean
  before declaring the attempt complete. If the land flow is still dropping
  evidence, surface that as a test failure, not a silent leak.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go` OR a new
  `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_evidence_test.go` — regression tests.

## Files NOT to Touch

- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` lines 875-960 — the per-bead
  instructions text (`executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText` /
  `executeBeadInstructionsAgentText`). Those are agent-facing and must keep
  telling the agent to `git add <specific-paths>` only. The fix belongs in
  the orchestrator, not the prompt: DDx wrote the evidence, DDx commits it.
- `AGENTS.md`, `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md`,
  `skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md` — the policy is already correct as of commit
  3a2a0c5. This bead implements the policy; it does not amend it.

## Cross-Bead Context

- Parent epic `ddx-32b3008e` ("Evolve execute-bead into a tracked
  execution-evidence workflow", P0, spec-id FEAT-006) is the meta for this
  work. This bead is the first execution-layer task under that epic.
- The trail-preservation policy is codified in commit 3a2a0c5: AGENTS.md
  "Merge Policy" section, FEAT-006 "Execution Trail Preservation in
  Downstream Merges" section (with 3 acceptance criteria), and
  skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md "Branch Merge Policy" section.
- The land flow today uses a mix of `git merge --no-ff` and
  `git update-ref` (for bare-repo worktrees) — see
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:166-544 for the three code paths
  that touch the merge. Any of them may be the right insertion point; pick
  the one that runs LAST after the agent commit is finalized.
- The bug-fix commit 2792dfc ("anchor SessionLogDir to project root") gives
  the orchestrator a reliable absolute path for session logs. This bead is
  the analogous fix for execution evidence.

## Spec Excerpt (FEAT-006 Execution Trail Preservation, normative)

> **Execution Trail Preservation in Downstream Merges** [...]
> Bead records store `closing_commit_sha` as a pointer back into git
> history; routing, cost, latency, retry-count, and tier-escalation reports
> read these commits directly. The trail realizes the `output = bead(input)`
> contract the system is built on: every bead attempt is recoverable from
> git history alone.
>
> **Acceptance Criteria (execution trail preservation):**
> - Given a branch containing at least one `chore: update tracker
>   (execute-bead` commit, when the branch is merged to its target, then
>   every commit SHA on the feature branch remains reachable from the target
>   branch HEAD after the merge.
> - Given a bead has `closing_commit_sha` set, when the branch containing
>   that commit is merged to main, then `git cat-file -e
>   <closing_commit_sha>` still succeeds on main after the merge.

This bead adds a fourth, implementation-layer criterion: that the
per-attempt evidence dir is *present in the trail at all*, not merely
preserved across merges.

  (or its immediate predecessor in the same merge).

- Scan .ddx/executions/*/result.json for harness and outcome fields
- Group by harness (or harness+model if useful) 
- Calculate success rate = count(task_succeeded) / count(all)
- Include avg cost and duration per tier

.ddx/executions/20260416T044613-0f04e8ce/manifest.json:14:    "description": "## Scope\n\nexecute-bead's orchestrator currently leaves the per-attempt evidence files it\nmaterializes (`.ddx/executions/\u003cattempt-id\u003e/manifest.json`, `prompt.md`,\n`result.json`, `checks.json`, `usage.json`) untracked after the agent run\nreturns. Successful bead commits reach main via the \"Merge bead\" path, but the\nevidence sits in the working tree unstaged and orphaned. This bead closes that\ngap: after the agent finishes, DDx (not the agent) stages the evidence and\nreturns the working tree clean.\n\nConcrete evidence of the gap: commit 98e379b on main is a one-shot backfill\nthat imports 254 historical `.ddx/executions/\u003cts\u003e-\u003chash\u003e/` directories\n(2026-04-11 → 2026-04-15) that were never staged by the execute-bead pipeline.\nThat backfill must not need to happen again.\n\n## Files to Touch\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go` — the land/merge flow. After the\n  agent's commit is produced (or after `Merge()` / bare-ref update), and\n  before returning `ExecuteBeadReport`, stage the per-attempt evidence dir and\n  fold it into the merge. Two shapes are acceptable:\n  (a) amend the evidence into the bead's own commit (agent's commit + DDx's\n      evidence files, single commit), OR\n  (b) emit a trailing `chore: add execution evidence [\u003cattempt-id\u003e]` commit\n      on the working branch as a sibling to the bead's commit, in the same\n      merge transaction.\n  Choose (b) if amending the agent's commit would rewrite its SHA — the agent\n  commit's SHA is referenced by `session_id` / `closing_commit_sha` and must\n  not move. Choose (a) only if the evidence can be staged BEFORE the agent\n  commits (e.g. the agent is told via `git add` hooks to pick up a DDx-seeded\n  index). Pragmatic recommendation: (b).\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` — the per-run orchestrator entry. After\n  the executor returns, verify the working tree is clean; if it is not,\n  diagnose untracked files introduced by this run, stage them into the\n  trailing evidence commit (if they are under `.ddx/executions/\u003cattempt\u003e/`),\n  and fail the run with a clear error otherwise (\"orchestrator left worktree\n  dirty: \u003cpath\u003e\").\n- `cli/internal/server/workers.go` — the server-worker path (`runWorker`,\n  `buildAgentRunner`). Same contract applies: after `ExecuteBead` returns and\n  `LandBeadResult` is called, the worker must verify the worktree is clean\n  before declaring the attempt complete. If the land flow is still dropping\n  evidence, surface that as a test failure, not a silent leak.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go` OR a new\n  `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_evidence_test.go` — regression tests.\n\n## Files NOT to Touch\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` lines 875-960 — the per-bead\n  instructions text (`executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText` /\n  `executeBeadInstructionsAgentText`). Those are agent-facing and must keep\n  telling the agent to `git add \u003cspecific-paths\u003e` only. The fix belongs in\n  the orchestrator, not the prompt: DDx wrote the evidence, DDx commits it.\n- `AGENTS.md`, `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md`,\n  `skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md` — the policy is already correct as of commit\n  3a2a0c5. This bead implements the policy; it does not amend it.\n\n## Cross-Bead Context\n\n- Parent epic `ddx-32b3008e` (\"Evolve execute-bead into a tracked\n  execution-evidence workflow\", P0, spec-id FEAT-006) is the meta for this\n  work. This bead is the first execution-layer task under that epic.\n- The trail-preservation policy is codified in commit 3a2a0c5: AGENTS.md\n  \"Merge Policy\" section, FEAT-006 \"Execution Trail Preservation in\n  Downstream Merges\" section (with 3 acceptance criteria), and\n  skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md \"Branch Merge Policy\" section.\n- The land flow today uses a mix of `git merge --no-ff` and\n  `git update-ref` (for bare-repo worktrees) — see\n  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:166-544 for the three code paths\n  that touch the merge. Any of them may be the right insertion point; pick\n  the one that runs LAST after the agent commit is finalized.\n- The bug-fix commit 2792dfc (\"anchor SessionLogDir to project root\") gives\n  the orchestrator a reliable absolute path for session logs. This bead is\n  the analogous fix for execution evidence.\n\n## Spec Excerpt (FEAT-006 Execution Trail Preservation, normative)\n\n\u003e **Execution Trail Preservation in Downstream Merges** [...]\n\u003e Bead records store `closing_commit_sha` as a pointer back into git\n\u003e history; routing, cost, latency, retry-count, and tier-escalation reports\n\u003e read these commits directly. The trail realizes the `output = bead(input)`\n\u003e contract the system is built on: every bead attempt is recoverable from\n\u003e git history alone.\n\u003e\n\u003e **Acceptance Criteria (execution trail preservation):**\n\u003e - Given a branch containing at least one `chore: update tracker\n\u003e   (execute-bead` commit, when the branch is merged to its target, then\n\u003e   every commit SHA on the feature branch remains reachable from the target\n\u003e   branch HEAD after the merge.\n\u003e - Given a bead has `closing_commit_sha` set, when the branch containing\n\u003e   that commit is merged to main, then `git cat-file -e\n\u003e   \u003cclosing_commit_sha\u003e` still succeeds on main after the merge.\n\nThis bead adds a fourth, implementation-layer criterion: that the\nper-attempt evidence dir is *present in the trail at all*, not merely\npreserved across merges.\n\n## Verification Commands\n\n- `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent -run TestExecuteBeadLandsEvidence`\n  passes (new regression test, see AC below).\n- `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent ./internal/server ./cmd` passes\n  (full suite; no regressions).\n- Manual dogfooding: in a throwaway clone, run\n  `ddx agent execute-loop --once` against a ready bead. After it returns,\n  `git status --porcelain` MUST print nothing. `git log -1 --stat` MUST show\n  at least one file under `.ddx/executions/\u003cattempt\u003e/` in the HEAD commit\n  (or its immediate predecessor in the same merge).",

.ddx/executions/20260416T212529-c6fa6346/prompt.md:5:## Scope
Add ddx agent metrics tier-success command that aggregates execution outcomes by harness/model tier.

## Context
Execution evidence result.json records harness, concrete model (via manifest.json or result.json), outcome (task_succeeded/task_failed/etc.), cost_usd, and duration_ms. This command aggregates those into tier-level success rates to inform routing policy.

## Files to touch
- cli/cmd/agent_metrics.go — new subcommand under ddx agent metrics
- cli/cmd/agent.go — register metrics subcommand group
- cli/cmd/agent_metrics_test.go — test with fixture execution dirs

## Implementation notes
- Scan .ddx/executions/*/result.json for harness and outcome fields
- Group by harness (or harness+model if useful) 
- Calculate success rate = count(task_succeeded) / count(all)
- Include avg cost and duration per tier

- The trail-preservation policy is codified in commit 3a2a0c5: AGENTS.md
  "Merge Policy" section, FEAT-006 "Execution Trail Preservation in
  Downstream Merges" section (with 3 acceptance criteria), and
  skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md "Branch Merge Policy" section.
- The land flow today uses a mix of `git merge --no-ff` and
  `git update-ref` (for bare-repo worktrees) — see
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:166-544 for the three code paths
  that touch the merge. Any of them may be the right insertion point; pick
  the one that runs LAST after the agent commit is finalized.
- The bug-fix commit 2792dfc ("anchor SessionLogDir to project root") gives
  the orchestrator a reliable absolute path for session logs. This bead is
  the analogous fix for execution evidence.

## Spec Excerpt (FEAT-006 Execution Trail Preservation, normative)

> **Execution Trail Preservation in Downstream Merges** [...]
> Bead records store `closing_commit_sha` as a pointer back into git
> history; routing, cost, latency, retry-count, and tier-escalation reports
> read these commits directly. The trail realizes the `output = bead(input)`
> contract the system is built on: every bead attempt is recoverable from
> git history alone.
>
> **Acceptance Criteria (execution trail preservation):**
> - Given a branch containing at least one `chore: update tracker
>   (execute-bead` commit, when the branch is merged to its target, then
>   every commit SHA on the feature branch remains reachable from the target
>   branch HEAD after the merge.
> - Given a bead has `closing_commit_sha` set, when the branch containing
>   that commit is merged to main, then `git cat-file -e
>   <closing_commit_sha>` still succeeds on main after the merge.

This bead adds a fourth, implementation-layer criterion: that the
per-attempt evidence dir is *present in the trail at all*, not merely
preserved across merges.

## Verification Commands

- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run TestExecuteBeadLandsEvidence`
  passes (new regression test, see AC below).
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent ./internal/server ./cmd` passes
  (full suite; no regressions).
- Manual dogfooding: in a throwaway clone, run
  `ddx agent execute-loop --once` against a ready bead. After it returns,
  `git status --porcelain` MUST print nothing. `git log -1 --stat` MUST show
  at least one file under `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/` in the HEAD commit
  (or its immediate predecessor in the same merge).

I also need to confirm the bead’s dependency state before I touch code, because the description calls out a blocking upstream artifact schema change.

data.output:
# Work — Drain the Queue, Execute, Verify, Close

"Doing work" in DDx means draining the ready queue: pick the top ready bead,
run one or more `ddx try` attempts, verify the result, and close the bead on
success or leave it available for a future eligible retry.

## Default surface: `ddx work`

```bash
ddx work
```

`ddx work` drains the queue by picking ready beads and invoking `ddx try`.
`ddx try` wraps `ddx run`, which is the single agent invocation primitive. DDx
owns queue iteration, attempt evidence, and retry policy; the upstream agent
owns provider/model routing.

Flags worth knowing:

- `--once` — pick one bead and stop (don't loop).
- `--poll-interval <dur>` — continuous worker mode; wait between
  iterations.
- `--min-power <n>` / `--max-power <n>` — requested agent power bounds.
- `--top-power` — choose a `MinPower` threshold from the agent catalog.
- `--harness <name>` / `--provider <name>` / `--model <ref>` — passthrough
  constraints only. DDx sends them unchanged and does not route on them.

## Primitive: `ddx try`

For targeted re-runs, debugging, or running a specific bead:

```bash
ddx try <bead-id>
ddx try <bead-id> --from <rev>      # base commit override
ddx try <bead-id> --no-merge        # preserve result, don't land
```

`ddx try` runs one bead attempt in an isolated git worktree. It calls `ddx run`
for the actual agent invocation.

## Pick by default, not by ID

Under normal operation, **don't specify a bead ID**. `ddx work` picks
the top ready bead based on priority + dependency satisfaction. Only
pin a specific ID when debugging (`ddx try <id>`) or
when the queue ordering would pick the wrong bead and you need to
override.

## Verify independently before closing

**Agents hallucinate successful completions.** Do not trust the
agent's self-report. Before closing a bead:

1. Run the acceptance-criteria command yourself (from the bead's
   `accept:` field). If it's `go test ./foo/...`, run that.
2. Check the resulting commit against the in-scope file list from
   the bead description. Out-of-scope files touched? Reject the
   attempt.
3. Read the commit message — does it reference the bead ID?
   (`[ddx-<id>]` or similar in the commit subject is the convention.)

If all three pass: close the bead.

## Close on success, unclaim on failure

DDx try/work outcomes form a specific taxonomy. Each outcome
maps to a concrete follow-up action:

| Outcome | Meaning | Action |
|---|---|---|
| `success` | Tests pass, AC met, commit landed | `ddx bead close <id>` |
| `already_satisfied` | No changes needed (AC was already green) | `ddx bead close <id>` |
| `no_changes` | Agent returned without producing commits and wrote `no_changes_rationale.txt` | Leave open, **unclaim** |
| `no_evidence_produced` | Agent exited without a commit or rationale | Leave open, **unclaim**, investigate harness/commit failure |
| `land_conflict` | Merge conflict on landing the result | Leave open, **unclaim** |
| `post_run_check_failed` | Tests or gate failed after landing | Leave open, **unclaim**, investigate |
| `execution_failed` | Agent subprocess errored (timeout, crash, provider error) | Leave open, **unclaim** |
| `structural_validation_failed` | Result failed structural sanity check | Leave open, **unclaim**, investigate |

`ddx work` applies these actions automatically. If you're running
`ddx try` directly, apply them manually: `ddx bead update <id>
--unclaim` to release a bead after a non-closing outcome, so another
worker can pick it up.

**Never leave a bead half-owned** — every execution either closes or
unclaims.

## Testing expectations for bead implementations

When an agent implements a bead, its output should include tests
that exercise the new code:

- **Unit tests** for logic with in-memory stubs for first-party
  collaborators. Favor stubs over mocks; mocks that assert on call
  sequences test implementation, not behavior.
- **Integration tests** against real collaborators where the cost
  is small (real git in a temp dir, real DB in a temp file, real
  HTTP via a local test server).
- **Real e2e tests** at the outermost boundary. E2e tests that mock
  the database or the network are unit tests lying about their
  scope.
- **Coverage measurement only where the project tracks it.** Don't
  introduce coverage tooling just for one bead; use what the
  project already has. Coverage is a signal, not a target.
- **Performance claims require baselines.** If the bead's AC
  mentions "faster" or "scales", the acceptance test must include:
  (a) a numeric baseline, (b) an explicit boundary (what's
  measured, what's excluded), (c) a reproducible harness.

## Anti-patterns

- **Trusting the agent's "done"**: always re-run the AC command
  yourself before closing.
- **Closing on no_changes/no_evidence**: only `success` and `already_satisfied`
  close a bead. `no_changes` requires an explicit rationale. `no_evidence_produced`
  means the agent returned without a commit or rationale — unclaim and investigate.
- **Squashing bead-attempt commits**: the per-attempt history is
  an audit trail (evidence commits, heartbeats). Use only
  `git merge --ff-only` or `--no-ff`; never squash/rebase/filter.
- **Running passthrough pins without a reason**: power-bound dispatch lets the
  agent choose an appropriate route. Use `--harness`, `--provider`, or
  `--model` only for explicit operator constraints, bug repros, or controlled
  tests.
- **Parallel workers on the same claimed bead**: the tracker
  guards against this via claim semantics, but don't try to defeat
  it — each claim represents an in-flight attempt.

## CLI reference

```bash
ddx work                                    # default queue drain
ddx work --once                             # one bead, then stop
ddx work --poll-interval 30s                # continuous worker
ddx work --min-power 10                     # request stronger attempts
ddx work --harness claude                   # passthrough constraint

ddx try <id>                                # one bead attempt
ddx try <id> --from <rev>                   # override base commit
ddx try <id> --no-merge                     # preserve iteration
```

Full flag list: `ddx work --help`, `ddx try --help`, `ddx run --help`.

ddx:
  id: FEAT-008
  depends_on:
    - helix.prd
    - FEAT-002
    - FEAT-014
---
# Feature: DDx Server Web UI

**ID:** FEAT-008
**Status:** In Progress
**Priority:** P1
**Owner:** DDx Team

## Overview

The DDx server (`ddx-server`) serves a web UI for browsing artifacts, beads,
the artifact dependency graph, and DDx run activity. Artifacts include markdown
documents plus sidecar-identified media such as diagrams, wireframes, images,
and PDFs. The UI is a SvelteKit application built with Svelte 5, embedded into
the Go binary via `embed.FS`, and served alongside the MCP and HTTP API
endpoints from a single process.

`ddx-server` runs as a per-user host daemon (one instance per machine, see
FEAT-002 and FEAT-020), so the same UI enumerates every project that the
host+user registry knows about and binds its navigation to one selected
project context at a time. The node-aware URL structure and combined
cross-project dashboards are specified in FEAT-021; FEAT-008 owns the
underlying views that those routes render.

The UI consumes GraphQL operations defined in `cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql`.
Every page lists its required query or subscription by name for traceability.

## Problem Statement

**Current situation:** DDx artifacts, beads, and the artifact graph are only accessible via CLI commands or MCP tool calls. There's no visual way to browse the artifact library, see the dependency graph, check bead status, regenerate generated artifacts, or review layer-aware run evidence.

**Pain points:**
- CLI output for `ddx doc graph` is text-only — dependency graphs need visual rendering
- Bead status and dependency trees are hard to grok in terminal output
- DDx run activity has no browsable interface that preserves the `work` -> `try` -> `run` layers
- Non-markdown artifacts have no type-aware browser preview, even when the graph knows their identity
- Generated artifacts have no UI affordance for checking provenance or requesting regeneration
- New team members can't quickly understand what artifacts exist or how they relate

**Desired outcome:** A web dashboard served from the same `ddx-server` binary that already handles MCP and HTTP API. Developers open a browser, see their artifact library, dependency graph, bead board, generated-artifact provenance, and DDx run activity — all from one URL.

## Architecture

### Single Binary, Multiple Surfaces

```
ddx-server binary
├── /            → Web UI (embedded SvelteKit)
├── /api/        → HTTP REST API (JSON) — frozen
└── /graphql     → GraphQL endpoint (gqlgen)
```

The SvelteKit frontend queries the `/graphql` endpoint for all data. REST endpoints
remain unchanged and are consumed by the CLI and MCP tools only.

### Project Context

The UI is rendered under the node-scoped URL structure defined in FEAT-021:
`/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/...` for project-scoped views and
`/nodes/:nodeId/...` for combined node-wide dashboards. The root `/`
redirects to `/nodes/:nodeId` using the ID returned by `GET /api/node`
(FEAT-020). When more than one project is registered the navigation bar
exposes a project picker populated from `GET /api/projects`; selecting a
project swaps the `:projectId` segment in place. All GraphQL queries issued by the
UI are bound to the selected project context.

### Project Navigation Tabs

Each `/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/...` page exposes the following
project-scoped tabs in the global navigation, in this order:

| Tab | Route segment | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| Overview | (none) | Project home, recent activity, operator-prompt input (ADR-021) |
| Beads | `beads` | Bead list / kanban / ready / detail (US-082*) |
| Artifacts | `artifacts` | Artifact browser (US-080, US-081b, US-083) |
| Graph | `graph` | Document dependency graph (US-081, US-081c) |
| Runs | `runs` | Layer-aware run history with `work` / `try` / `run` chips, row expansion, and re-queue (US-086b, US-086c) |
| Workers | `workers` | Live + recent worker processes for this project (US-085b, US-086, US-086a) |
| Personas | `personas` | Persona browser + role bindings (US-097) |
| Plugins | `plugins` | Plugin registry + install/uninstall (US-098) |
| Commits | `commits` | Project git commit log (US-093, FEAT-021) |
| Efficacy | `efficacy` | Model efficacy + comparisons (US-096) |

**Sessions and Executions are retired as top-level tabs** (Story 8). The
unified Runs tab carries layer chips and inline row expansion that
surface what those tabs used to show:

- `layer=run` rows expand to AgentSession transcript / billing / cached
  tokens / stderr (the old Sessions detail).
- `layer=try` rows expand to the `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/` bundle
  metadata, check results, and verdict (the old Executions detail).
- `layer=work` rows expand to queue inputs and child run links.

**URL deprecation policy.** During the deprecation window, the legacy
URLs respond with **302 redirects (NOT 301)** to the matching filtered
Runs URL with all query params preserved, plus a `Sunset` response
header that names the cutoff date for the redirects:

| Legacy URL | Redirect target |
|---|---|
| `/sessions` | `/runs?layer=run` |
| `/sessions#<id>` | `/runs?layer=run&session=<id>` |
| `/executions[?bead=&verdict=&harness=&q=&after=]` | `/runs?layer=try` with the original query string preserved verbatim |
| `/executions/[id]` | `/runs/[runId]?bundle=true` |

302 (not 301) is intentional: bookmarks and external link aggregators
should not cache the redirect permanently while we still own the legacy
routes. The redirect response carries `Sunset: <RFC 9745 date>` and the
project nav drops the Sessions/Executions entries once the redirects and
the internal-link sweep land (`shellRoutes.ts`, `BeadDetail.svelte`,
commits page, agent page, Workers detail).

### Build Pipeline

```
cli/internal/server/frontend/   → SvelteKit + TypeScript
  bun run build                 → cli/internal/server/frontend/build/
cli/internal/server/            → Go with embed.FS
  go build                      → ddx-server (single binary with embedded UI)
```

During development, SvelteKit's dev server proxies `/graphql` to the running Go server.

### Technology Choices

| Layer | Choice | Why |
|-------|--------|-----|
| Frontend framework | **Svelte 5** | Compile-time optimizations, no Virtual DOM |
| Build tool | **SvelteKit** | Router, layout, API routes, hooks, adapter-static for embedding |
| Styling | **Tailwind CSS** | Consistent with website (Hextra uses Tailwind) |
| Data fetching | **graphql-request + graphql-ws** | Lightweight GraphQL client with typed queries and WebSocket subscriptions |
| Graph visualization | **D3.js** or **Cytoscape.js** | For document dependency graph rendering |
| Embedding | **Go embed.FS** | Compile frontend into the binary — no external files needed |
| Go HTTP | **Chi** or **net/http** | Standard library compatible routing |
| Go GraphQL | **gqlgen** | Schema-first, code-generated resolvers |

### Alternatives Considered

**Templ + htmx** — simpler (no JS toolchain), but the dependency graph visualization and interactive bead board need more interactivity than htmx provides well. Templ + htmx is great for CRUD; we need a richer graph/dashboard experience.

**Local-first with CRDT sync** — interesting for offline use but overkill for v1. The UI is primarily read-heavy (browsing docs, viewing graphs). Can be added later with PowerSync or ElectricSQL if needed.

## Requirements

### Functional

1. **Artifact browser, viewer, editor, and regeneration**
   - List artifacts by category (prompts, personas, patterns, templates, ADRs,
     SDs, diagrams, wireframes, generated images, PDFs)
   - Full-text search across textual artifact content, sidecar metadata, and
     markdown frontmatter
   - View markdown artifacts rendered as markdown with syntax highlighting
   - Render artifacts by `media_type`:
     - `text/markdown`: markdown renderer with syntax-highlighted code blocks
     - `image/svg+xml` with Mermaid provenance: sanitized inline SVG preview
     - `application/vnd.excalidraw+json`: Excalidraw read-only embed
     - `image/*`: inline image preview with metadata fallback
     - `application/pdf`: embedded PDF preview with open-file fallback
     - unknown or binary media: metadata panel plus open/download affordance
   - Show `ddx:` frontmatter or sidecar `.ddx.yaml` metadata (id,
     `media_type`, dependencies, `generated_by`, and staleness status)
   - Clickable dependency links — navigate upstream/downstream docs inline
   - **Editor**: in-browser markdown editing with save-to-disk (calls API to write file)
   - Sidebar showing document dependency tree for current doc
   - Staleness indicator per document (fresh/stale/missing-deps)
   - Generated-artifact provenance indicator: show producing run id, prompt or
     generator summary, and source-hash status when `generated_by` is present
   - Generated-artifact staleness is artifact-local: a `generated_by.source_hash`
     mismatch marks only the generated artifact stale and does not cascade like
     `depends_on`
   - Regenerate action for generated artifacts calls the `artifactRegenerate`
     mutation, returns the new run id, and links directly to the produced run
     detail and updated artifact

2. **Document dependency graph**
   - Interactive visual graph (D3/Cytoscape) showing all document relationships
   - Color-code nodes by staleness (green=fresh, orange=stale, red=missing-deps)
   - Click node to navigate to document detail
   - Filter by document type, staleness status
   - Zoom/pan for large graphs
   - Layout options (hierarchical top-down, force-directed)

3. **Bead views**

   The beads UI runs entirely in the browser. On load, the client fetches the
   full bead set from the `beads` query (GraphQL). All search, filter, sort, and graph
   traversal happens client-side using an in-browser data layer.

   **Master-detail list view:**
   - Searchable list: full-text across title, description, acceptance, labels
   - Sortable columns: ID, title, status, priority, labels, owner, updated_at
   - Filterable by status, priority, label, owner
   - Click a bead to open detail panel (split pane or slide-over)
   - Detail shows: all fields, dependency tree, execution beads, agent sessions
   - Graph traversal: click a dependency → navigate to that bead's detail
   - Drill-down into run records linked to the bead (shows layer, pass/fail,
     logs, duration)

   **Kanban board view:**
   - Columns represent status: `open`, `in_progress`, `closed`
   - Cards within columns ordered by priority (P0 top, P4 bottom)
   - Dependency grouping: beads that block each other cluster visually
     (similar to Trello, Fuzz by 37signals, GitHub Projects)
   - Drag-and-drop to change status (calls `beadUpdate` mutation)
   - Swimlanes option: group rows by label (e.g., `area:cli`, `area:web`)
   - Color coding: priority → card border, labels → chips, blocked → dimmed
   - Collapsed card shows: title, priority badge, label chips, dep count
   - Expanded card shows: description, acceptance, execution summary

   **Ready queue view:**
   - Filtered view showing only beads with all dependencies satisfied
   - Sorted by priority, then by creation date

ID:       ddx-e7b80a50
Title:    review: server session schema/store/lifecycle + manifest+turns.jsonl evidence contract
Type:     task
Status:   in_progress
Priority: 2
Labels:   phase:2, story:18, area:server, kind:feature
Owner:    erik
Parent:   ddx-42b917fe
Deps:     ddx-cd2ecf79
Desc:     PROBLEM
No server-side ReviewSession model, persistence layer, or schema exists. Story 18 review mutations and the Story 16 run-detail page both depend on a well-defined review session schema and persistence contract. Without this, both are blocked.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/ contains no ReviewSession struct or persistence layer.
- .ddx/reviews/ directory does not exist as a managed path (no mkdir, no lifecycle helpers).
- The Story 16 page (ddx-4cd64068) needs to read review turns from .ddx/reviews/<session-id>/turns.jsonl; the contract must be agreed before both 16 and 18 can land without merge conflicts.
- ddx-cd2ecf79 (artifact type spec, a dep) must land first for {template, review_prompt, preferred_reviewer} fields.

PROPOSED FIX
- Define ReviewSession struct (fields: id, artifact_id, artifact_sha, artifact_git_rev, system_rubric, template_ref, prompt_ref, status, turns[], cost_usd, max_billable_usd) in cli/internal/server/review_session.go.
- Implement persistence under .ddx/reviews/<session-id>/:
  - manifest.json: session metadata (id, status, cost, refs).
  - turns.jsonl: append-only JSONL of turn records (actor, content, cost_usd, created_at).
  - attachments/: binary attachments (if any).
- Document the manifest.json + turns.jsonl contract explicitly as the Story 16 contract.

NON-SCOPE
- GraphQL mutations (ddx-de278b8d).
- Turn dispatcher (ddx-f7c3d512).
- Frontend rendering.
Accept:   1. ReviewSession struct defined in cli/internal/server/review_session.go with all required fields.
2. .ddx/reviews/<session-id>/manifest.json written on session create with correct schema.
3. .ddx/reviews/<session-id>/turns.jsonl written as append-only JSONL.
4. manifest.json + turns.jsonl format documented in source (comment block) as the Story 16 contract.
5. TestReviewSession_CreatePersistsManifest verifies manifest.json written with correct fields.
6. TestReviewSession_AppendTurn_TurnsJsonl verifies turn appended to turns.jsonl.
7. TestReviewSession_RoundTrip verifies schema deserialization round-trip.
8. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... green.
9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

data.text: I’m adding a dedicated review-session store now, not wiring this through the broader server state, because the contract is a standalone on-disk schema and that keeps the tests tight. The initial write will create the session directory, write `manifest.json`, preseed an empty `turns.jsonl`, and use an append-only writer for later turns.
I’ve got the store in place. I’m adding direct filesystem tests next so the ACs prove the on-disk contract, not just the Go object shape.
I caught one compile issue in the new test file and I’m also widening the JSONL reader buffer before I run `go test`, because review turns can be much larger than the default scanner token limit.

The implementation is in place. I’m formatting and then running the narrow server-package tests first so I can fix any structural issues before I expand to the full `internal/server` suite and pre-commit gate.

The code is compiling far enough to start the test runs. I’m watching the focused review-session tests and the broader server suite in parallel so I can catch either a local contract bug or an unrelated package regression quickly.
